summary: The Australian Financial Complaints Authority (AFCA) has released new guidelines for insurers on how to handle complaints related to non-disclosure or misrepresentation in insurance applications. These guidelines require insurers to provide detailed documentation to support their underwriting decisions, making it harder to justify changes after a claim is made.
ticker_line: AFCA sets new standards for insurance complaints
author: https://www.insurancebusinessmag.com/au/authors/roxanne-libatique/
heading: General Insurance

title: APRA reshapes leadership as prudential reforms gather pace
summary: The Australian Prudential Regulation Authority (APRA) has appointed two new deputy chairs, Therese McCarthy Hockey and David Bradbury, to help manage its growing supervisory workload. These changes come as APRA implements important reforms to improve operational resilience in the financial sector, while Suzanne Smith will continue to oversee the insurance sector.
ticker_line: APRA appoints two new deputy chairs for reforms
author: https://www.insurancebusinessmag.com/au/authors/roxanne-libatique/
heading: Regulation

title: Insurer margins tighten as government targets specialist fees
summary: Australian health insurers are facing tighter profit margins as the government pushes for clearer specialist fees and billing practices. This comes amid rising out-of-pocket costs for patients and new regulations that could impact insurers' pricing and operations.
ticker_line: Health insurers face tighter margins amid reforms
author: https://www.insurancebusinessmag.com/au/authors/roxanne-libatique/
heading: Health Insurance

title: ART, AustralianSuper deliver strong FY26 returns
summary: Australian Retirement Trust (ART) and AustralianSuper reported strong investment returns for FY26, with ART's High Growth option returning 9.21% and AustralianSuper's High Growth option returning 11.58%. Both funds credited their performance to diversified portfolios and strong global equity markets despite ongoing geopolitical uncertainty.
ticker_line: ART and AustralianSuper report solid FY26 returns
author: Vinny Vucago
heading: Superannuation

title: Zurich enters home insurance market through Honey partnership
summary: Zurich Financial Services Australia will enter the home insurance market by partnering with Honey Insurance, starting in October 2026. Zurich will manage claims for Honey's home, landlord, and motor insurance, aiming to improve customer experience in a challenging insurance landscape.
ticker_line: Zurich partners with Honey for home insurance
author: https://www.insurancebusinessmag.com/au/authors/daniel-wood/
heading: General Insurance

title: ASIC Daily News round-up - 3 July 2026
summary: The Federal Court has fined ASX Limited $20.5 million for misleading statements about its CHESS replacement project. Additionally, ASIC has released the results of the June 2026 Financial Advisers Exam.
ticker_line: ASX hit with $20.5 million penalty for misleading conduct
author: "ASIC Media Unit" <media@newsletters.asic.gov.au>
heading: ASIC

title: Tribunal backs public access to NSW home building premium calculations
summary: A tribunal ruled that details on how home building premiums in NSW are calculated must be made public, but icare's internal business data will remain confidential. The decision, made on June 29, 2026, aims to address concerns over high premiums in a monopolised market.
ticker_line: Tribunal orders disclosure of premium calculation methods
author: https://www.insurancebusinessmag.com/au/authors/tez-romero/
heading: General Insurance

title: ASX ordered to pay $20.5 million penalty for misleading conduct relating to CHESS replacement project
summary: The Federal Court has fined ASX Limited $20.5 million for misleading statements about its CHESS replacement project, which caused confusion about its progress.
ticker_line: ASX hit with $20.5 million penalty for misleading conduct
author: "ASIC Media Unit" <media@newsletters.asic.gov.au>
heading: ASIC

title: APRA releases National Claims and Policies Database statistics for December 2024
summary: The Australian Prudential Regulation Authority (APRA) has published updated statistics for the National Claims and Policies Database (NCPD) as of December 2024. This database includes information on professional indemnity and liability insurance claims from general insurers in Australia.
ticker_line: APRA updates National Claims Database statistics
author: APRA web updates <subscriptions@apra.gov.au>

title: What AI 'doom trolling' means for workforce planning
summary: AI leaders warn of job losses while promoting their technology, creating challenges for HR leaders. They must plan carefully to avoid negative impacts on the workforce and ensure employees are supported during changes.
ticker_line: AI leaders warn of job losses amid tech growth

heading: AI

title: Media release: Saipem Subsea7 merger requires Phase 2 review
summary: The ACCC has decided that the proposed merger between Saipem and Subsea7 needs a detailed Phase 2 review due to concerns it may reduce competition in the market.

author: ACCC Media <do-not-reply@accc.gov.au>
heading: ACCC

title: The 247% case for fixing claims handling and why it mostly isn’t happening
summary: A new report by EML highlights three key steps to improve claims handling for injured workers, showing that timely support and better communication can significantly increase their chances of returning to work. Despite evidence of success, these practices are rarely implemented, leading to ongoing issues in the claims process.
ticker_line: EML calls for better claims handling practices
author: https://www.insurancebusinessmag.com/au/authors/daniel-wood/
heading: General Insurance

title: Travel insurance under pressure as school holiday travellers fly out
summary: As winter school holidays begin, Australian families are buying travel insurance for trips abroad and domestically. However, concerns about global conflicts and pandemics are making travellers cautious about what their policies cover.
ticker_line: Travel insurance faces new challenges this holiday season
author: https://www.insurancebusinessmag.com/au/authors/daniel-wood/
heading: General Insurance

title: APRA welcomes new Deputy Chairs
summary: The Australian Prudential Regulation Authority (APRA) has appointed Therese McCarthy Hockey and David Bradbury as new Deputy Chairs. Their terms start on July 9 and September 1, respectively, and they will help oversee APRA's work in banking and superannuation.
ticker_line: APRA appoints new Deputy Chairs for oversight
author: APRA web updates <subscriptions@apra.gov.au>
heading: APRA

title: Media release: New bans on water market manipulation and insider trading now in force
summary: New laws have been introduced to prevent market manipulation and insider trading in the Murray-Darling Basin water markets. The ACCC now has more power to ensure transparency and tackle misconduct in water trading.
ticker_line: New laws ban water market manipulation and insider trading
author: ACCC Media <do-not-reply@accc.gov.au>
heading: ACCC

title: Half the home insurance market now prices bushfire mitigation
summary: Half of Australia's home insurers now consider bushfire safety measures when setting prices, leading to premium reductions for some households. This change follows the inclusion of NAB Insurance and CommBank Insurance in a government-backed resilience scheme aimed at improving affordability and risk management.
ticker_line: Home insurers factor in bushfire safety measures
author: https://www.insurancebusinessmag.com/au/authors/roxanne-libatique/
heading: General Insurance

title: ANZIIF summit reflects changing insurance priorities
summary: The ANZIIF will hold its first International Insurance Summit in February 2027, focusing on key issues like AI governance, climate resilience, and operational challenges in the insurance industry. The event aims to bring together over 900 delegates to discuss how these factors are shaping the future of insurance.
ticker_line: ANZIIF to host first International Insurance Summit
author: https://www.insurancebusinessmag.com/au/authors/roxanne-libatique/
heading: General Insurance

title: Severe weather renews focus on Australia's catastrophe exposure
summary: Severe weather across southern Australia has renewed concerns about the rising costs of insurance due to climate-related risks, with South Australia's emergency services responding to heavy rain and flooding. The Australian Prudential Regulation Authority warns that ongoing severe weather could lead to more households being uninsured by 2050.
ticker_line: Severe weather raises insurance cost concerns
author: https://www.insurancebusinessmag.com/au/authors/roxanne-libatique/
heading: General Insurance

title: CSLR levy jumps by another $60 million
summary: The CSLR levy for financial advisers will rise to nearly $200 million for the 2027 financial year, up $63 million from earlier estimates. This increase is due to a higher number of expected claims, particularly related to the failures of Dixon Advisory and the Shield and First Guardian products.
ticker_line: CSLR levy for advisers to hit $200 million
author: https://www.facebook.com/smstrusteenews

title: AMP, CFS, MLC post double-digit returns
summary: AMP Super, Colonial First State, and MLC have reported strong annual returns for their members, with AMP achieving over 11% returns for the third year in a row, CFS delivering double-digit gains for the fourth consecutive year, and MLC also showing solid performance despite market volatility.
ticker_line: AMP, CFS, and MLC report strong super returns

summary: ASIC has issued stop orders against two products from Stratfund's Australian Fixed Income Fund to protect consumers. It also cancelled Capital Guard's financial licence for selling fake bonds and launched an improved company search service.

title: Update from the Treasury
summary: The Treasury has published updates on three key topics: a consultation on regulating accounting and consulting firms, a government response to a Senate report on bank closures in regional areas, and the final report of an independent review of the Enhanced Regulatory Sandbox, all released on July 1, 2026.

author: The Treasury <no-reply@treasury.gov.au>

title: ASIC issues DDO stop orders against Stratfund’s Australian Fixed Income Fund
summary: The Australian Securities and Investments Commission (ASIC) has issued temporary stop orders on two products from Stratfund's Australian Fixed Income Fund. This action aims to protect consumers from investing in products that may not meet their financial needs.

heading: ASIC

title: Fire and ISR reinsurance is outrunning the claims it covers
summary: Reinsurance costs for Australia's fire and industrial special risks insurance are significantly higher than claims paid out, putting pressure on insurers' profits. This trend raises concerns about the sustainability of insurance pricing, especially if another catastrophic event occurs.

author: https://www.insurancebusinessmag.com/au/authors/daniel-wood/

title:
Heather Loewenthal appointed Independent Chair of the General
 Insurance Code Governance Committee
summary: Heather Loewenthal has been appointed as the Independent Chair of the General Insurance Code Governance Committee. She will oversee the monitoring of insurers' compliance with the General Insurance Code of Practice, following a joint nomination by the Australian Financial Complaints Authority and the Insurance Council of Australia.
ticker_line: Heather Loewenthal named Chair of Insurance Code Committee
author: Medianet Press Release <medianet@releases.medianet.com.au>
heading: AFCA

title: Media release: Lactalis pays penalties over alleged misleading ‘fresh’ milk claims
summary: Lactalis Australia has paid $59,400 in penalties for misleading claims on its 'Country Fresh' and 'WA DAIRY FRESH' milk products, which were labelled as fresh but contained reconstituted ingredients. The ACCC issued three infringement notices against the company for these false representations.
ticker_line: Lactalis fined for misleading milk labels
author: ACCC Media <do-not-reply@accc.gov.au>
heading: ACCC

title: Payday Super to increase retirement by $5k for younger members
summary: New Payday Super reforms will help young workers save up to $5,200 more for retirement by ensuring super contributions are paid with wages instead of quarterly. This change aims to reduce unpaid super and allow for quicker investment of retirement savings.
ticker_line: Payday Super boosts retirement savings for young workers

heading: Superannuation

title: ASIC cancels AFS licence of Capital Guard for fake bond sale and other dishonest conduct
summary: ASIC has revoked the Australian Financial Services licence of Capital Guard AU Pty Ltd due to dishonest actions, including selling a fake bond and providing false documents to its auditor.
ticker_line: ASIC cancels Capital Guard's financial licence
author: "ASIC Media Unit" <media@newsletters.asic.gov.au>
heading: ASIC

title: Australia takes cyclone pool lessons to OECD forum
summary: Australia's Cyclone Reinsurance Pool was highlighted at an OECD forum as a successful example of how governments and insurance markets can collaborate to manage climate risks and improve insurance affordability. The scheme aims to support households and businesses in cyclone-prone areas while maintaining incentives for risk reduction.
ticker_line: Australia showcases cyclone insurance model at OECD forum
author: https://www.insurancebusinessmag.com/au/authors/roxanne-libatique/
heading: General Insurance

title: NIBA questions cyclone pool’s wider impact
summary: The National Insurance Brokers Association (NIBA) has raised concerns that the Cyclone Reinsurance Pool only helps a small part of the insurance market, while many Australians find home insurance unaffordable. NIBA is calling for a broader review of competition and costs in the insurance sector following a report from the ACCC that highlighted rising insurer profits and ongoing issues in Western Australia.

author: https://www.insurancebusinessmag.com/au/authors/roxanne-libatique/
heading: General Insurance

title: APRA carves out cyber, management liability as standalone data
summary: The Australian Prudential Regulation Authority (APRA) will publish cyber insurance and management liability as separate categories in its National Claims and Policies Database, aiming to improve data for better pricing. This change, effective from July 1, 2026, addresses the growing market but raises concerns about individual insurers being identifiable due to limited data.
ticker_line: APRA separates cyber and management liability data
author: https://www.insurancebusinessmag.com/au/authors/roxanne-libatique/
heading: Regulation

title: Division 296 status must be reported
summary: SMSF accountants must report if a fund member is subject to Division 296 tax rules, especially if they have over $3 million in their fund. Failure to do so may lead to claims of misleading the ATO, depending on the accountant's knowledge of the client's financial situation.
ticker_line: Accountants must report Division 296 status to ATO
author: https://www.facebook.com/smstrusteenews
heading: Superannuation

title: Payday Super will boost balances
summary: The new Payday Super system starts today, aiming to increase superannuation balances for workers by ensuring more timely and frequent payments. This change is expected to boost average retirement savings by about $5,200 for young workers and over $9,000 for average workers, while also helping to reduce unpaid super contributions.
ticker_line: New Payday Super system boosts retirement savings
author: https://www.facebook.com/smstrusteenews
heading: Superannuation

title: Final report into Life Code makes 85 recommendations
summary: An independent review of the Life Insurance Code has proposed 85 recommendations to improve mental health support, customer protection, and claims handling. The review, led by Peter Kell, aims to enhance trust in the life insurance industry and will be considered by the Council of Australian Life Insurers by September 2026.
ticker_line: Life Insurance Code review suggests 85 changes
author: Matthew Wai
heading: Life Insurance

title: Treasury looks to reform regulation on consulting firms
summary: Treasury is proposing new rules for accounting and consulting firms to improve accountability after KPMG's misuse of client data. Options include requiring licenses from ASIC and separating audit services from consulting to reduce conflicts of interest.
ticker_line: Treasury plans reforms for consulting firm regulations
author: Riddhima Talwani
heading: Regulation

title: Nat cat cover to stay profitable if industry keeps innovating – Fitch
summary: A Fitch Ratings report shows that less than 2% of people in climate-exposed areas have natural catastrophe insurance, despite expected claims rising to $186 billion by 2030. The report stresses that the insurance industry must innovate to close the growing protection gap, especially in emerging markets, or face long-term losses.
ticker_line: Fitch warns of growing insurance protection gap
author: https://www.insurancebusinessmag.com/au/authors/josh-recamara/
heading: General Insurance

title: Investors back active management
summary: An independent review of the Life Insurance Code of Practice has proposed 85 recommendations to improve areas like mental health support and claims handling. Meanwhile, Treasury is looking to regulate consulting firms better after issues with KPMG's handling of client data.
ticker_line: Life Insurance Code review suggests major changes
author: Vinny Vucago
heading: Life Insurance

title: AI companies urged to pay up or stay out of Australia
summary: AI companies are being urged to pay for using Australian news content or stay out of the country. The Digital Publisher Alliance calls for government support for local media and warns that AI firms threaten the industry by not compensating for the work they use.
ticker_line: AI firms must pay for news content in Australia
author: Jennifer Dudley-Nicholson
heading: Regulation

title: A Once-Promising AIG insurance venture in China on sale for 15 Cents
summary: PICC Life Insurance is selling its joint venture with AIG for just 15 cents, but the buyer must also pay over $1.8 million in debts. This sale reflects ongoing challenges in China's insurance sector, with regulators tightening oversight and larger insurers being recapitalised.
ticker_line: PICC Life sells AIG venture for 15 cents
heading: Life Insurance

title: Mega-fund dominance raising fresh systemic risk concerns
summary: Australia's superannuation sector is becoming dominated by a few large funds, which could increase financial risks despite their ability to support banks during crises. APRA warns that this concentration could lead to significant impacts on the financial system as more people retire and withdraw funds.

author: Adrian Suljanovic
heading: Superannuation

title: Rest urged overhaul of super performance test benchmarks
summary: Rest superannuation fund is calling for changes to the government's performance test, saying it limits investment in new asset classes that could benefit members. They propose a new benchmark for emerging assets and better protections for members in accumulation products.

author: Adrian Suljanovic
heading: Superannuation

title: Aware Super lifts retirement living exposure
summary: Aware Super is buying Lendlease's 25.1% stake in Keyton, raising its ownership to 75%. This move strengthens Aware Super's position in Australia's growing retirement living sector, which has 66 villages and over 15,000 residents.
ticker_line: Aware Super boosts stake in Keyton to 75%
author: Adrian Suljanovic
heading: Superannuation

title: APRA releases response to consultation on the National Claims and Policies Database
summary: The Australian Prudential Regulation Authority (APRA) has announced its final decisions on updates to the National Claims and Policies Database (NCPD), including a new way to publish statistics. These changes will be implemented as planned.
ticker_line: APRA updates National Claims Database publication format
author: APRA web updates <subscriptions@apra.gov.au>
heading: APRA

title: Media release: ACCC welcomes new mobile coverage map standard and warns on misleading coverage claims
summary: The ACCC supports a new standard for mobile coverage maps that requires providers to show clear and accurate 4G and 5G coverage across Australia. They also warn companies against making misleading claims about their network coverage.
ticker_line: ACCC introduces new standards for mobile coverage maps

summary: The ACCC supports a new standard for mobile coverage maps that requires providers to show accurate 4G and 5G coverage across Australia. They also warn companies against making misleading claims about their network coverage.

summary: The ACCC has blocked Coles Supermarkets from acquiring a new supermarket and liquor site in Kalgoorlie-Boulder, Western Australia, citing concerns over competition.

author: ACCC Media <do-not-reply@accc.gov.au>

title: Australia ranks second globally in QS future skills index as AI reshapes...
summary: Australia ranks second in the QS World Future Skills Index 2027, showing strong readiness for an AI-driven job market, just behind the United States. The country needs to keep improving education and training to meet the growing demand for skills in technology and AI.

title: D&O insurance is more profitable than its reputation suggests
summary: Directors and officers (D&O) insurance in Australia is showing strong profitability after a tough period, with consistent positive results and stabilising premiums. As competition increases, brokers can offer better coverage at lower costs, but clients should be cautious of new risks like AI and cyber liabilities.

title: Life insurers allowed to limit, not drop, mental health cover
summary: Australian life insurers can limit, but not eliminate, mental health cover in standard policies, according to a new report. The report outlines guidelines for insurers to follow, including the need for documented support for any limitations and a review every three years.

author: https://www.insurancebusinessmag.com/au/authors/roxanne-libatique/

title: Global insurance premiums climb 7.1% as growth cools
summary: Global insurance premiums grew by 7.1% in 2025, reaching €6.9 trillion, but growth has slowed from previous years. Life insurance remains the largest segment, while health insurance saw the strongest growth at 12.3%, driven by ageing populations and rising medical costs.

title: LRBA ban addresses past problems
summary: The government plans to restrict limited recourse borrowing arrangements (LRBAs) in self-managed super funds (SMSFs) for buying residential properties, based on a 2014 inquiry. author: https://www.facebook.com/smstrusteenews

title: ASIC pushes to bolster competitiveness
summary: ASIC is working with market leaders to improve Australia's capital markets and keep pace with global financial innovations. A new report highlights the need for modernised infrastructure and collaboration to support safe growth and competitiveness in the financial sector.

title: Super system to hit $12.4tn by 2045

summary: The Australian Prudential Regulation Authority (APRA) found that the superannuation sector is crucial for financial stability, especially during severe stress scenarios. Despite facing significant liquidity pressures, super funds managed to meet member demands while highlighting the need for better risk management and valuation policies.

title: ASIC Daily News round-up - 30 June 2026
summary: Giuseppe DeFrancesco, a restaurateur from NSW, has been charged by ASIC with three offences related to his actions at Jasa Dining Pty Ltd, which is now in liquidation. ASIC is also pushing for stronger competitiveness in Australian financial markets during a roundtable event in Sydney.

author: "ASIC Media Unit" <media@newsletters.asic.gov.au>

title: NSW restaurateur Giuseppe DeFrancesco first person charged by ASIC with creditor defeating criminal offences
summary: Giuseppe DeFrancesco, a restaurateur from NSW, has been charged by ASIC with three offences related to his work at Jasa Dining Pty Ltd, which is in liquidation. This marks the first time ASIC has charged someone with creditor defeating criminal offences.
ticker_line: NSW restaurateur charged with serious offences
author: "ASIC Media Unit" <media@newsletters.asic.gov.au>
heading: ASIC

title: APRA releases Monthly Authorised Deposit-taking Institution Statistics for May 2026
summary: The Australian Prudential Regulation Authority (APRA) has published its Monthly Authorised Deposit-taking Institution Statistics for May 2026, which can be accessed on their website.

title: APRA farewells Deputy Chair Margaret Cole
summary: Margaret Cole has completed her five-year term as Deputy Chair of APRA, where she improved oversight of the superannuation industry and enforcement efforts. APRA Chair John Lonsdale thanked her for her contributions and noted that the government will announce the recruitment process for her replacement soon.
ticker_line: APRA bids farewell to Deputy Chair Margaret Cole
author: APRA web updates <subscriptions@apra.gov.au>
heading: APRA

title: APRA publishes findings of inaugural System Risk Stress Test
summary: APRA's first System Risk Stress Test shows that Australia's banks and superannuation funds can handle severe financial shocks. The test revealed both strengths and vulnerabilities in the financial system, highlighting the need for better preparedness and potential changes to bank liquidity rules.
ticker_line: APRA stress test shows resilience in finance sector
author: APRA web updates <subscriptions@apra.gov.au>
heading: APRA

title: ASIC pushes for coordinated action to strengthen competitiveness of Australian markets
summary: ASIC is calling for a united effort from market leaders to improve the competitiveness of Australia's financial markets. This comes after research shows Australia risks falling behind as other countries quickly adopt new financial innovations.
ticker_line: ASIC urges action to boost market competitiveness
author: "ASIC Media Unit" <media@newsletters.asic.gov.au>
heading: ASIC

title: Media release: Amazon in court for introducing ads to Prime Video using allegedly unfair contract terms
summary: The ACCC has taken Amazon to court, claiming it used unfair contract terms in its Prime subscriptions to add ads to Prime Video, violating Australian Consumer Law.
ticker_line: ACCC sues Amazon over Prime Video ads
author: ACCC Media <do-not-reply@accc.gov.au>
heading: ACCC

title: Ransomware groups are coming for law firms
summary: Ransomware attacks on law firms surged in 2025, with average demands rising 60% to A$930,000 and incidents increasing by 54%. New reporting rules for ransomware payments are now in effect, adding complexity for affected firms.
ticker_line: Ransomware demands soar for law firms
author: https://www.insurancebusinessmag.com/au/authors/roxanne-libatique/
heading: Regulation

title: Clyde & Co finds AI adoption outpacing governance frameworks
summary: A survey shows that the rapid adoption of AI in Australia is outpacing the governance frameworks needed to manage it, leading regulators like APRA to warn banks and insurers about increasing technology risks. Many companies acknowledge they are struggling to keep up with the fast changes in AI and cybersecurity requirements.
ticker_line: AI adoption outpaces governance in Australia
author: https://www.insurancebusinessmag.com/au/authors/roxanne-libatique/
heading: Regulation

title: General insurance complaints surge as add-on insurance exposure resurfaces
summary: Complaints about general insurance in Australia rose 17% in FY2025, mainly due to issues with add-on insurance. The Australian Financial Complaints Authority received 34,231 complaints, with add-on insurance making up 23% of them, highlighting ongoing problems in the industry.
ticker_line: Surge in insurance complaints driven by add-ons
author: https://www.insurancebusinessmag.com/au/authors/roxanne-libatique/
heading: General Insurance

title: Future of SMSF lending in limbo
summary: The Australian government plans to ban self-managed super funds (SMSFs) from using certain loans to buy residential property, affecting new loans but allowing existing ones to continue. This change, part of a deal with the Greens, aims to improve housing affordability but raises concerns about investment options for younger and lower-income Australians.
ticker_line: Government to ban SMSF loans for homes
author: https://www.brokernews.com.au/authors/kellie-ell/
heading: Superannuation

title: $2.46 billion for reinsurance in one quarter - is the structure still fit for purpose?
summary: In the March 2026 quarter, Australia's general insurance industry spent $2.46 billion on reinsurance, raising questions about the effectiveness of current protection strategies as the industry still faced significant losses. Experts suggest that insurers need to reassess their reinsurance purchases and risk management to better handle severe events.
ticker_line: Reinsurance costs soar, losses still persist
author: https://www.insurancebusinessmag.com/au/authors/daniel-wood/
heading: General Insurance

title: ASIC Daily News round-up - 29 June 2026
summary: Trent Bowden, a former director in WA, has pleaded guilty to deceiving investors out of over $1.5 million. Meanwhile, Mercer Super has been fined $10.3 million for failing to report serious issues, and ASIC is urging superannuation trustees to improve protections for retirement savings.
ticker_line: Trent Bowden pleads guilty to investor fraud
author: "ASIC Media Unit" <media@newsletters.asic.gov.au>

author: The Treasury <no-reply@treasury.gov.au>
heading: Treasury

title: NSW workers compensation enters new era with dual guideline update
summary: New South Wales is updating its workers compensation guidelines starting June 30 and July 1, 2026, to address rising costs, particularly from psychological claims. The changes aim to tighten eligibility for claims and freeze premium rates, impacting insurers, brokers, and employers across the state.
ticker_line: NSW updates workers compensation guidelines for 2026

heading: Regulation

summary: GMHBA has appointed Jarrod Coysh, former CEO of REI Super, as its new CEO starting in the first quarter of the 2026-27 financial year. This change comes as the health insurer faces rising out-of-pocket costs for members, despite achieving the lowest premium increase in the industry for 2026.
ticker_line: GMHBA appoints new CEO from superannuation sector
author: https://www.insurancebusinessmag.com/au/authors/roxanne-libatique/
heading: Health Insurance

title: Life CCC fills consumer seat with financial services lawyer
summary: Anna Meulman, a consumer lawyer, will join the Life Insurance Code Compliance Committee on July 1, 2026, replacing Alexandra Kelly. Her appointment comes as the committee reviews the Life Insurance Code and aims to strengthen compliance and enforcement powers for insurers.

title: WA director Trent Bowden pleads guilty to over $1.5 million investor deception
summary: Trent Bowden, a former director from Seville Grove, WA, has pleaded guilty to three charges of misusing his position to deceive investors, resulting in losses of over $1.5 million. This case highlights serious misconduct in corporate governance.

author: "ASIC Media Unit" <media@newsletters.asic.gov.au>
heading: ASIC

title: Media release: Online travel agency eDreams pays penalties and gives undertaking over allegedly misleading subscription prices
summary: Online travel agency eDreams has paid penalties and agreed to change its practices after being accused of misleading customers about subscription prices. The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (ACCC) is warning people about potential scams related to this issue.

author: ACCC Media <do-not-reply@accc.gov.au>
heading: ACCC

title: ASIC secures $10.3 million in penalties against Mercer Super for systemic reporting failures
summary: The Federal Court has ordered Mercer Super to pay $10.3 million in penalties for failing to report serious issues, including charging insurance premiums after members had died. This decision highlights significant reporting failures within the company.
ticker_line: Mercer Super fined $10.3 million for reporting failures
author: "ASIC Media Unit" <media@newsletters.asic.gov.au>
heading: ASIC

title: APRA releases quarterly authorised deposit-taking institution statistics for March 2026
summary: The Australian Prudential Regulation Authority (APRA) has published its quarterly report for March 2026, showing a rise in net profit and total assets for authorised deposit-taking institutions. New lending increased significantly, particularly for investment loans with high debt-to-income ratios.
ticker_line: APRA reports strong growth in bank profits
author: APRA web updates <subscriptions@apra.gov.au>
heading: APRA

title: Media release: ACCC to monitor fuel prices and market behaviour as fuel excise is partly restored
summary: The ACCC will monitor fuel prices as the government reduces the fuel excise cut from 32 cents to 16 cents per litre from July 1 to August 2, 2026. They have warned fuel retailers not to exploit this change to raise prices unfairly for consumers.
ticker_line: ACCC to monitor fuel prices amid excise changes
author: ACCC Media <do-not-reply@accc.gov.au>
heading: ACCC

title: APRA consults on changes to bank risk weights designed to support lending and productivity
summary: The Australian Prudential Regulation Authority (APRA) is consulting on changes to bank risk weights to encourage lending while keeping banks financially strong. Proposed changes include lowering risk weights for infrastructure, unrated corporate loans, and residential property development to boost banks' lending capacity.
ticker_line: APRA proposes changes to boost bank lending
author: APRA web updates <subscriptions@apra.gov.au>
heading: APRA

title: Winners shine as industry turns out in force
summary: The Australian insurance industry celebrated its top achievers at the 2026 Insurance Business Australia Awards on May 1, with over 500 attendees. Key winners included Lisa Carter as CEO of the Year and NTI for Claims Team of the Year, highlighting the industry's resilience and commitment to excellence.
ticker_line: Insurance industry celebrates top achievers at awards

title: ASIC calls platform trustees to account over persistent failures to safeguard super savings
summary: ASIC is warning superannuation trustees to fix ongoing issues in protecting retirement savings. These include problems with monitoring fees, unusual charges, and risky investment activities.
ticker_line: ASIC demands better protection for super savings

title: CBA director warns financial crisis 'likely' amid rising alarm bells
summary: Harrison Young, a former director at Commonwealth Bank, warns that another financial crisis is likely due to rising risks in the global system. The Australian Prudential Regulation Authority has also raised concerns about financial institutions' preparedness for geopolitical shocks amid increasing global volatility.
ticker_line: CBA director warns of likely financial crisis

heading: Banking

title: FSC links active super choices to stronger retirement outcomes
summary: New research shows that Australians who actively manage their superannuation can boost their retirement savings significantly. Simple actions like switching to lower-fee or higher-growth options can lead to much larger retirement balances, especially for those under 50 who may currently be missing out on growth investments.

title: Over-65 PHI rebate cut draws unified sector pushback
summary: The Australian government plans to cut private health insurance rebates for those aged 65 and over, which has led to strong opposition from health sector groups. They warn this change could harm older Australians and increase costs for public hospitals, with potential annual costs rising by over $1,000 for some couples.

summary: John Gordon Owenell has cancelled his registration as a company auditor after the Australian Securities and Investments Commission (ASIC) raised concerns about his independence and potential conflicts of interest. This decision follows allegations of non-compliance with the Corporations Act and professional ethics standards.

summary: ASIC is suing former directors and compliance committee members of Keystone Asset Management for allegedly risking hundreds of millions of dollars of Australians' superannuation by investing funds without proper safeguards. The case is now in the Federal Court.

She emphasised the importance of transparency and accountability in superannuation, noting that these changes have benefited many Australians and helped improve performance outcomes for super fund members.

summary: The Australian Prudential Regulation Authority (APRA) is investigating Diversa Trustees Limited over its executive pay decisions to ensure they meet regulatory standards. This inquiry will check if the pay practices align with the rules set for superannuation trustees, but it will not look into member losses linked to the collapse of First Guardian.

This initiative aims to address the rising mental health issues in rural communities, offering various support tools at no extra cost to eligible policyholders.

This change is part of ASIC's ongoing effort to simplify regulatory processes, with more updates expected in the future.

This gap presents an opportunity for insurers to improve their visibility in AI-generated responses before competitors take the lead.

summary: ASIC has raised concerns about car finance providers, focusing on issues with third-party distributors, sales practices, and how they monitor customer outcomes. This comes as part of their efforts to ensure fair practices in the car finance industry.

title: The industry holds $17 billion plus above its minimum but one quarter shows how fast that can change
summary: The Australian general insurance sector has a strong capital surplus of $17.7 billion above its minimum requirement, but recent catastrophe losses have shown how quickly this can change. The industry is currently financially stable, but ongoing natural disasters could impact its capital position further.

author: https://www.insurancebusinessmag.com/au/authors/daniel-wood/

summary: The NSW Budget shows the Emergency Services Levy (ESL) will cost policyholders $1.5 billion in 2026-27, a 66% increase over five years. The Insurance Council of Australia calls for reform to shift funding from insurance premiums to a property-based levy, which could lower costs for many insured property owners.

author: https://www.insurancebusinessmag.com/au/authors/roxanne-libatique/

summary: NIBA will launch a young professionals program at its October convention to support early-career brokers, who currently make up only 11% of the workforce. This initiative aims to help new brokers connect, learn, and grow in the industry.

author: https://www.insurancebusinessmag.com/au/authors/roxanne-libatique/

summary: Therese McCarthy Hockey from APRA spoke at the AFIA Risk Summit about the urgent need for banks and insurers to address risks from advanced AI and quantum computing. She highlighted the importance of sharing information on vulnerabilities to improve security across the financial sector.

heading: Superannuation

summary: ASIC has raised concerns about car finance providers, focusing on issues with third-party distributors, sales practices, and how they monitor consumer outcomes. This move aims to ensure better protection for consumers in the car finance market.

author: "ASIC Media Unit" <media@newsletters.asic.gov.au>

summary: Australia's spy agency warns that AI-driven cyber attacks are imminent, posing a significant risk to businesses. Despite a profitable cyber insurance market, many companies remain underinsured as costs from cyber crime rise sharply.

author: https://www.insurancebusinessmag.com/au/authors/mark-rosanes/

This move is part of IAG's strategy to provide earlier intervention and support beyond traditional insurance claims.

author: https://www.insurancebusinessmag.com/au/authors/roxanne-libatique/
heading: General Insurance

title: Media release: Grill'd in court for allegedly misleading customers about 'Tree Day Tuesday' donations
summary: The ACCC is taking Grill'd to court for allegedly misleading customers about its 'Tree Day Tuesday' donations, claiming the company falsely stated it would donate $1 from each burger sold on Tuesdays to plant trees. This case is based on actions between January 2021 and April 2024, which the ACCC says violates Australian Consumer Law.
ticker_line: Grill'd faces court over misleading donations
author: ACCC Media <do-not-reply@accc.gov.au>
heading: ACCC

title: Marsh strikes deals with five insurers to simplify cyber payouts
summary: Marsh Risk has launched a new framework with five insurers to speed up cyber insurance payouts, allowing for advance payments and clearer guidelines on claims. This aims to reduce costs and simplify the claims process for businesses affected by cyber incidents.
ticker_line: Marsh simplifies cyber insurance payouts with new framework
author: https://www.insurancebusinessmag.com/au/authors/mark-rosanes/
heading: General Insurance

title: Flood-hit Bundaberg faces an insurance market in retreat
summary: Bundaberg, Queensland, is facing a crisis as home insurance becomes unaffordable after severe flooding. The local council rejected a $175 million levee project, leading to increased premiums and many residents lacking flood coverage.
ticker_line: Bundaberg faces rising insurance costs after floods
author: https://www.insurancebusinessmag.com/au/authors/roxanne-libatique/
heading: General Insurance

title: Old processes driving member change
summary: Many Australians are leaving their super funds for alternatives like SMSFs due to outdated paper processes and poor user experience. Experts say super funds need to improve their services to keep members engaged and reduce outflows.

heading: Superannuation

title: Home insurance is in structural crisis and APRA numbers tell you exactly how bad it is
summary: Australia's home insurance market is facing a crisis, with premiums rising sharply but insurers still reporting significant losses due to extreme weather events and rising building costs. Many homeowners are becoming uninsured or underinsured as affordability issues grow.

author: https://www.insurancebusinessmag.com/au/authors/daniel-wood/

title: ASFA lifts comfortable retirement standard to $730k for couples
summary: The Association of Superannuation Funds of Australia (ASFA) has raised the comfortable retirement savings benchmark to $730,000 for couples and $630,000 for singles, while many Australians believe they will need over $1 million to retire comfortably. Rising costs due to inflation and housing affordability issues are influencing these perceptions.

heading: Superannuation

title: Willis bets on data to close property insurance's growing climate gap
summary: Willis is improving its Climate Diagnostic tool to help property insurers better understand climate risks and protect assets from extreme weather. This comes as global insurance losses from natural disasters rise, with many properties underinsured, especially in the UK.

author: https://www.insurancebusinessmag.com/uk/authors/mark-rosanes/
heading: General Insurance

summary: ASIC has permanently banned Abdullah Popal from financial services due to fraud convictions. Additionally, ASX admitted to misleading the market about its CHESS replacement project, which could have caused financial harm.

title: ASIC permanently bans Abdullah Popal from financial services and credit
summary: The Australian Securities and Investments Commission (ASIC) has permanently banned Abdullah Popal from working in financial services and credit due to his fraud convictions. This decision aims to protect consumers from potential harm.

author: APRA web updates <subscriptions@apra.gov.au>

title: Four in ten overestimate retirement savings needed
summary: A new poll shows that 42% of Australians think they need over $1 million in superannuation for a comfortable retirement, while actual benchmarks are $730,000 for couples and $630,000 for singles. Rising living costs and housing affordability concerns are influencing these beliefs, especially among younger Australians.

heading: Superannuation

title: Media release: Ugg boot retailer pays penalties for strikethrough pricing claims
summary: Ugg boot retailer Christofi Investments has paid $39,600 in penalties for misleading pricing claims on its websites. The ACCC found that the company advertised discounts alongside inflated original prices, which were not accurate.

heading: ACCC

title: ASX admits misleading conduct relating to CHESS replacement project
summary: ASX Limited has admitted that its February 2022 statement about the CHESS replacement project was misleading, which could have put investors at financial risk. This admission highlights concerns over the project's progress.

heading: ASIC

title: The easy money is gone: What cyber’s soft market means for every broker with SME clients

author: https://www.insurancebusinessmag.com/au/authors/daniel-wood/

title: APRA sets longevity capital reporting template in motion
summary: APRA will allow life insurers to use a new capital calculation method for longevity products starting July 1, 2026. This method includes an optional factor to better align capital requirements with the long-term nature of these products, despite some industry concerns about specific rules.

title: QBE fills group board seat from within its own ranks

author: https://www.insurancebusinessmag.com/au/authors/roxanne-libatique/
heading: General Insurance

title: Where AI meets PI: Coverage questions the industry is still answering

They note that traditional insurance products may not adequately cover new risks associated with AI.
ticker_line: AI reshapes professional indemnity insurance landscape

title: Super funds race to implement digital advice

title: Media release: Hismile pays penalties for false and misleading social media videos

title: Victoria moves closer to mandating apartment defect insurance

title: Bushfire season no longer waits for summer, but Australians do

summary: A new TAL report shows that while 90% of retirees are happy with their pension choices, only 38% of pre-retirees understand these products. Many pre-retirees want more information from their super funds to help them plan better for retirement.

summary: The Federal Court has ruled that Express Online Training misled consumers about its RSA and White Card courses, following a case brought by the ACCC. The company made false claims regarding these online training programs.

AFCA authorised as External Dispute Resolution service for Scams
 Prevention Framework
summary: The Australian Financial Complaints Authority (AFCA) has been approved as the main service for handling scam complaints under the new Scams Prevention Framework. title: Super age limit interpretation strict
summary: A financial team has clarified that superannuation contributions can only be made by individuals under 75 years old during the year they turn 75. This means that even if someone is born on July 1, they cannot make contributions on that day, as they are considered 75 from midnight.

heading: Superannuation

title: ASX sees trade volumes soar in May
summary: The ASX reported a 31% rise in trade volumes in May, with average daily trades increasing to over 2.6 million. However, IPO activity remained slow, with no net growth as new listings were offset by delistings.

summary: Older Australians aged 55 to 75 made up half of all complaints to super funds in 2025, with the most complaints from those aged 55 to 64. The Australian Securities and Investments Commission (ASIC) reported that super funds paid out $12.1 million in remedies for various issues, mainly service delays and claims handling.

heading: Superannuation

author: Beth Musselwhite

summary: The ACCC has called for a stronger Horticulture Code after Fruitico and Fresh Express each paid $99,000 in penalties for not following the rules. Fruitico was found to have traded with grape growers without a proper agreement in place.

title: Research claims $450bn in unwanted super fund investments
summary: A report reveals that up to $450 billion of Australians' superannuation savings may be invested in industries like fossil fuels and weapons that many members want to avoid. The findings highlight a gap between what Australians expect from their super funds and where their money is actually invested.

title: Super funds confront liquidity, AI and data challenges
summary: Australian super funds are facing challenges with complex investments, liquidity demands, and the use of technology like AI, according to a new survey. As they invest more in private markets, managing risks and data has become crucial for their strategies.

author: Adrian Suljanovic

title: Advisers face retirement product complexity surge as client understanding lags
summary: Advisers are facing challenges with new retirement products as clients struggle to understand their options. Education is crucial for clients to feel confident in using lifetime income products alongside traditional superannuation options.

heading: Superannuation

title: =?utf-8?Q?APRA=20finalises=20new=20IRB=20accreditation=20pathway=20for=20banks?=
summary: APRA has introduced a new, easier pathway for banks to gain accreditation for the internal ratings-based (IRB) approach to calculate credit risk. This change aims to improve competition among banks while ensuring financial safety and encouraging better risk management practices.

author: APRA web updates <subscriptions@apra.gov.au>

title: Payments System Board Update: June 2026 Meeting
summary: The Payments System Board of the Reserve Bank of Australia met on June 4, 2026, to discuss the assessment of the RITS system, upcoming public consultations on payment regulations, and the need for stronger cryptographic protections in payments. They also noted findings from focus groups about Australians' payment needs and interest in digital money.

title: Push tardy funds to avoid Payday Super block
summary: SMSF clients must lodge their annual returns by June 12 to avoid their super contributions being redirected to other funds after July 1, as the new Payday Super system will reject late submissions.

title: ART seeks stronger First Nations member financial outcomes
summary: Australian Retirement Trust (ART) has launched a new plan to improve financial outcomes for First Nations members, aiming for better support and education by 2028. The plan includes hiring more First Nations staff and strengthening partnerships with Indigenous organisations.

heading: Superannuation

title: AMP Super expands retirement offering with income and care
summary: AMP has launched a new retirement income package for superannuation members, offering lifetime income, support for aged care, and digital financial advice. This initiative aims to help Australians manage their finances better as they approach retirement.

author: Adrian Suljanovic

title: Business
summary: The Australian Taxation Office is consulting on updates to GST rules for build-to-rent developments and tax incentives to clarify existing laws and improve guidance for stakeholders. Feedback is being gathered from industry groups to ensure the rules meet current needs.

title: =?utf-8?Q?Media=20release=3A=20ACCC=20approves=20Ampol=27s=20acquisition=20of=20EG=20Australia=2C=20subject=20to=20conditions?=
summary: The ACCC has approved Ampol's purchase of EG Australia, but Ampol must sell 41 fuel sites to an approved buyer to ensure competition. Ampol currently operates 576 sites, while EG Australia has 512.

title: AFCA on track to hit record high complaints in FY26
summary: The Australian Financial Complaints Authority (AFCA) is set to reach a record number of complaints in the 2025-26 financial year, with over 95,000 complaints received so far. Factors like the collapses of Shield and First Guardian have contributed to this increase, and AFCA is expanding its ability to handle more types of complaints.

title: Dependency being tightly applied
summary: The ATO is stricter on who can claim death benefits dependency, requiring ongoing and substantial financial support. Recent rulings show that many claims are being denied due to these tough criteria.

title: Super funds push back on CSLR: SMC
summary: The Super Members Council is asking the government to reject plans that would make superannuation funds pay for the Compensation Scheme of Last Resort, arguing that those responsible for financial misconduct should bear the costs instead. They also raised concerns about fairness and the need for better consumer protections.

author: Vinny Vucago
heading: Superannuation

title: =?utf-8?Q?Media=20release=3A=20Peter=20Warren=27s=20acquisition=20of=20Wakeling=20Automotive=20sites=20requires=20Phase=202=20review?=

title: Super funds prioritising resilience as volatility reshapes strategies
summary: Australian super funds are focusing on building resilient portfolios by investing more in private markets, infrastructure, and real estate due to economic uncertainties and the impact of artificial intelligence. They are also exploring AI as a productivity tool while facing challenges with ESG data quality.

title: 2024 Completed matters
summary: The Australian Taxation Office (ATO) has completed consultations on various tax matters in 2024, including updates on cloud services for payroll reporting and new public country-by-country reporting guidelines for multinational companies.

title: Advice under development – GST issues
summary: The Australian Taxation Office is developing new guidance on Goods and Services Tax (GST) issues, including clarifications on cross-border supplies, build-to-rent developments, care services in retirement villages, and recipient created tax invoices. These updates aim to provide clearer rules and support for taxpayers.

heading: ATO

title: Advice under development – not-for-profit sector issues
summary: The Australian Taxation Office is finalising guidance on when private and public ancillary funds can be said to provide a benefit, which affects their tax obligations. This guidance is expected to be completed by June 2026.

title: International exchange instruments list
summary: The Australian Taxation Office (ATO) has updated its list of international exchange instruments, which are agreements with foreign tax authorities to improve cooperation and information sharing. These include memorandums of understanding (MOUs) with countries like China, France, and Indonesia.

heading: ATO

title: Payments to Indian residents for technical services
summary: Payments made by Australian customers to Indian residents for certain remote technical services are no longer taxable in Australia, effective from 29 December 2022. This change is part of a law amendment linked to the Australia-India Economic Cooperation and Trade Agreement.

title: 2025 Completed matters
summary: The Australian Taxation Office (ATO) has completed consultations on various tax matters in 2025, including interest withholding tax exemptions and guidance for the new Payday Super legislation starting July 2026. These consultations aimed to gather industry insights to improve public advice and guidance for taxpayers.

title: Matters under consultation
summary: The Australian Taxation Office is consulting on various tax matters affecting individuals, businesses, not-for-profits, and superannuation, including the tax treatment of digital assets and new tax incentives for build-to-rent developments. These consultations aim to gather community input before final decisions are made.

summary: The Australian Taxation Office is developing new guidelines and determinations on capital gains tax (CGT) issues, including rules for back-to-back rollovers, cryptocurrency transactions, and deceased estates. These updates aim to clarify tax implications and are expected to be completed between June and late 2026.

title: Protect yourself from misinformation and disinformation
summary: The Australian Taxation Office warns about misinformation and disinformation related to tax and superannuation. They advise using trusted sources and checking information to avoid serious consequences.

heading: ATO

title: Superannuation
summary: The ATO is consulting on a new service called Payday Super, which allows employers to report unpaid superannuation for employees. This consultation aims to improve the process for lodging Voluntary Disclosure Statements to help reduce potential charges for late payments.
ticker_line: ATO seeks feedback on Payday Super service

title: Intermediaries

 insurance

title: ASIC flags disclosure flaws in first wave of mandatory climate reports
summary: The Australian Securities and Investments Commission (ASIC) has found issues in the first mandatory climate reports, including misleading disclaimers and inconsistent disclosures. While the overall quality has improved, ASIC warns that some companies are not fully disclosing climate risks or using unclear reporting methods.
ticker_line: ASIC flags issues in climate report disclosures
author: Vinny Vucago
heading: Regulation

title: ACCC approves Zurich's ClearView takeover
summary: The ACCC has approved Zurich Financial Services' takeover of ClearView Wealth for 65 cents per share, valuing the deal at about $425 million. The merger is expected to have a low impact on competition in the life insurance market in Australia.
ticker_line: ACCC approves Zurich's ClearView acquisition
author: Karren Vergara
heading: Life Insurance

title: NGS Super broadens advice offering with digital planning tools
summary: NGS Super has launched new digital tools and expanded financial planning services to help members access affordable advice earlier, aiming to improve engagement and reduce costs associated with financial guidance.
ticker_line: NGS Super enhances member advice with digital tools
author: Adrian Suljanovic
heading: Superannuation

title: Parliament passes survivor compensation super access laws
summary: The Albanese Government has passed a law allowing victims of child sexual abuse to access the superannuation of offenders if they have unpaid court-ordered compensation. This change aims to prevent perpetrators from hiding their assets and ensures accountability.
ticker_line: New law helps abuse survivors access superannuation
author: Adrian Suljanovic
heading: Superannuation

title: RBA and DFCRC Release Findings From Project Acacia
summary: The Reserve Bank of Australia and the Digital Finance Cooperative Research Centre released findings from Project Acacia, which explored how digital money and tokenised assets can improve Australia's wholesale financial markets. The project identified benefits and challenges of tokenisation and proposed a new program to support innovation in the financial sector.
ticker_line: RBA and DFCRC unveil Project Acacia findings
heading: RBA

title: CBA rout signals end of banks’ era on ASX: VanEck
summary: VanEck warns that the dominance of Australia's major banks, particularly the Commonwealth Bank, may be ending after a $30 billion drop in its market value. Investors should look beyond banks for future opportunities as economic conditions change.
ticker_line: End of banks' dominance on ASX signals change
author: Binaya Dahal
heading: Banking

title: ART overhauls death and TPD insurance fees
summary: Australian Retirement Trust (ART) is reducing insurance fees for death and Total & Permanent Disability (TPD) cover starting July 1, with average decreases of 14.1% for men and 31.2% for women. New fees and changes to TPD benefits will also be introduced, along with updates to investment risk labels and allocation strategies.
ticker_line: ART cuts insurance fees and updates benefits
author: Riddhima Talwani
heading: Superannuation

title: New trust tax may impact estate planning
summary: The SMSF Association warns that new tax rules on discretionary trusts, starting 1 July 2028, may affect estate planning strategies. While testamentary trusts created before the budget announcement will be exempt, uncertainties remain about future implications and potential opportunities for self-managed super funds (SMSFs).
ticker_line: New tax rules may change estate planning strategies
author: https://www.facebook.com/smstrusteenews
heading: Superannuation

title: Risk of wasting Div 296 capital losses
summary: SMSFs using Division 296 tax adjustments risk losing capital losses if they can't match them with gains. This could lead to wasted losses that can't be carried forward without corresponding gains.
ticker_line: SMSFs face risks with Division 296 capital losses
author: https://www.facebook.com/smstrusteenews
heading: Superannuation

title: AI adoption is high, but confidence and outcomes are low
summary: A report shows that while many Australian workers are using AI regularly, their confidence in its benefits is low. Only 13% expect AI to improve their job roles in the next year, highlighting a gap between adoption and productivity.
ticker_line: AI use rises, but confidence remains low
author: Daniel Croft
heading: AI

title: How much are the top super portfolio managers earning?
summary: A report shows superannuation portfolio managers earn between $220,000 and $350,000, with bonuses adding up to 50% of their salary. Despite low wage growth in finance, demand for experienced leaders and compliance professionals is rising due to industry pressures.
ticker_line: Super fund managers see high salaries amid demand
author: Adrian Suljanovic
heading: Superannuation

title: Australians back tougher crackdown on super switching advertisements
summary: A new survey shows that 80% of Australians want the government to ban misleading superannuation ads that encourage risky fund switches. The findings highlight the need for stronger protections after 12,000 people lost $1.2 billion due to the collapse of certain super funds.
ticker_line: Australians demand ban on risky super ads
author: Adrian Suljanovic
heading: Superannuation

title: Funds urged to boost insurance engagement among younger members
summary: A study by TAL shows that younger Australians and women often lack understanding of their insurance in superannuation. It suggests super funds should improve communication and guidance to help members feel more secure about their coverage.
ticker_line: Funds urged to engage younger members on insurance
author: Adrian Suljanovic
heading: Superannuation

title: Aware targets adviser demand with expanded technical education support
summary: Aware Super is boosting support for financial advisers by hiring Troy Smith as senior manager of technical services. He will lead education initiatives to help advisers with complex retirement planning and superannuation strategies.
ticker_line: Aware Super enhances adviser support with new hire
author: Adrian Suljanovic
heading: Superannuation

title: Digital ID program gets $650M for expansion in Australian federal budget
summary: The Australian government is investing $654.3 million in its 2026-27 budget to expand the Digital ID program, aiming to simplify identity verification for over 255 government services. This funding will enhance the Australian Government Digital ID System and improve data sharing and security measures.
ticker_line: Australia boosts Digital ID program with $654M investment
publication: Biometric Update | Biometrics News, Companies and Explainers
author: Joel R. McConvey
heading: Economy

title: High Street's restructure bets against the agency roll-up model
summary: High Street Underwriting Agency's new parent company, 11th Avenue, is taking a different approach to agency consolidation by keeping each agency's independence and decision-making intact, rather than merging them into a larger corporate structure.
ticker_line: High Street bets on agency independence amid consolidation
author: Daniel Wood
heading: General Insurance

title: A win delivered for super members
summary: The Australian government will keep current tax rules for superannuation, including SMSFs, which means no changes to capital gains tax for super funds. This decision is seen as a positive outcome for the 19 million Australians with super accounts, ensuring stability in their retirement savings.
ticker_line: Government maintains stable tax rules for superannuation
author: https://www.facebook.com/smstrusteenews
heading: Superannuation

title: ASIC funding boost welcomed
summary: The Super Members Council has welcomed a $17.8 million funding boost for ASIC in the 2026 federal budget to enhance investor protection and strengthen the superannuation system.
author: https://www.facebook.com/smstrusteenews
heading: Superannuation

title: Australian ETF market surges to record $346bn
summary: Australia's ETF market reached a record $346 billion in assets after a $16.6 billion increase in April, driven by strong investor inflows and a rebound in global equity markets.
author: Vinny Vucago
heading: Investments

title: Industry welcomes government's productivity push
summary: The Actuaries Institute and the Australian Banking Association have praised the government's Federal Budget measures aimed at enhancing productivity in the Australian economy, highlighting the importance of ongoing reforms for sustainable growth.
author: Riddhima Talwani
heading: Economy

title: Federal budget delivers wins and gaps for the insurance sector
summary: The 2026-27 Federal Budget includes measures affecting the insurance sector, such as a $3.4 million allocation for property insurance reforms, a doubled APRA approval threshold, and notable omissions regarding the Hazards Insurance Partnership.
author: Roxanne Libatique
heading: General Insurance

title: Ransomware hits Australian networks faster than ever before
summary: Ransomware attacks in Australia are escalating rapidly, with a significant reduction in the time from initial access to deployment, now averaging around 30 minutes, highlighting increased risks for organisations and insurers.
author: Roxanne Libatique
heading: AI

title: Allianz storms into 2026 as P&C arm powers blockbuster quarter
summary: Allianz SE reported a record first-quarter operating profit of €4.5 billion for 2026, driven by strong performance in its property-casualty and asset management sectors, while core net income surged 48.4% to €3.8 billion, largely due to one-off gains from divestments in India.
author: Kenneth Araullo
heading: General Insurance

title: Australia's most ambitious budget in decades – but have businesses been left wanting more?
summary: The Albanese Government's 2026-27 budget introduces significant tax cuts, wage reforms, and workforce investments aimed at addressing economic challenges, but reactions from business leaders vary.
author: Jack Campbell
heading: Economy

title: Budget delivers super stability but sector wants further action
summary: Australia's 2026-27 Federal Budget includes a $17.8 million consumer safety package for superannuation, while the sector calls for further reforms to enhance consumer protections and retirement outcomes.
author: Adrian Suljanovic
heading: Superannuation

title: Super reforms tipped to boost Australians’ retirement savings further
summary: Recent superannuation reforms in Australia, including Payday Super and expanded LISTO eligibility, are expected to significantly enhance retirement savings for many workers, particularly lower-income earners and parents on leave.
author: Adrian Suljanovic
heading: Superannuation

title: Federal budget backs AI with $70 million accelerator and major agency trials
summary: The Australian federal budget has allocated $70 million for an AI Accelerator program and announced various AI trials across government agencies aimed at enhancing productivity and efficiency.
author: Jennifer Dudley-Nicholson
heading: AI

title: ASIC to receive $10m in FY27 to improve MIS supervision
summary: The Australian government will allocate $10.3 million to ASIC in FY27 to enhance supervision of managed investment schemes, alongside additional funding for governance and fraud prevention measures.
author: Matthew Wai
heading: Regulation

title: Government plans 'standardised' assessment for NDIS
summary: The Australian government plans to implement a standardised assessment for the NDIS to improve service quality, clarify eligibility, and reduce costs by $37.8 billion over four years.
author: Riddhima Talwani
heading: General Insurance

title: Government to commit $3.7bn to strengthen aged care access
summary: The Australian government will invest $3.7 billion to enhance aged care access, including the construction of new facilities and improvements to existing services.
author: Matthew Wai
heading: Health Insurance

title: IAG sets sights on $25 billion in premiums by 2030
summary: Insurance Australia Group (IAG) aims to achieve over $25 billion in gross written premiums and 11 million customers by 2030 through its 'Ambition 2030' strategy, focusing on technology and strategic partnerships.
author: Jonalyn Cueto
heading: General Insurance

title: WorkSafe opens comment on claimants’ rights code
summary: Victorians can provide feedback on a draft Code of Claimants’ Rights until May 27, aimed at enhancing the treatment of injured workers under the workers' compensation scheme.
author: Jonalyn Cueto
heading: General Insurance

title: SMSFs leading super innovation
summary: The SMSF Association highlights the growing trend of cryptocurrency investments among self-managed super funds (SMSFs), indicating their role as innovators in the superannuation industry.
author: https://www.facebook.com/smstrusteenews
heading: Superannuation

title: ATO issues SAN misuse warning

author: https://www.facebook.com/smstrusteenews
heading: AI

title: Health groups warn budget rebate cuts threaten private cover

author: Jonalyn Cueto
heading: Health Insurance

title: Canvas hackers reach deal with Instructure

heading: AI

title: Rest faces scrutiny over alleged super reporting error

heading: Superannuation

heading: Investments

heading: Superannuation

title: ARPC’s 2025-26 cyclone tally is in – but not final

heading: General Insurance

heading: Superannuation

title: AUSTRAC releases updated risk snapshot of Australia’s financial crime landscape

Analysts suggest that the RBA may need to act preemptively to mitigate long-term inflation risks.
