title: RBA fears tax, rates will take the heat out of housing market
summary: The Reserve Bank of Australia's chief economist warns that new tax changes and rising interest rates will slow down the housing market. Inflation is also a concern, driven by global issues like the war in Iran, which could lead to an economic slowdown similar to the early 1990s recession.
ticker_line: RBA warns of housing market slowdown amid rising rates
author: Shane Wright and Mike Foley
heading: Economy

title: Exclusive: Blame budget pain on rates, war: treasurer
summary: NSW Treasurer Daniel Mookhey warns the state's economy will grow slowly for at least three years due to high interest rates and rising fuel prices from Middle East conflicts, affecting household budgets and state revenue. The government is cautious about future budget surpluses as economic forecasts remain uncertain.
ticker_line: NSW economy faces slow growth and budget concerns
author: Michael McGowan and Matt Wade
heading: Economy

title: ‘Use it or lose it’: Super tax break that is closing soon
summary: Australians have a limited time to make extra tax-deductible contributions to their superannuation before the financial year ends. Those with unused contribution caps can save on taxes, but must check their super fund's deadline to avoid missing out.
ticker_line: Last chance for super tax breaks this financial year
author: Anthony Keane
heading: Superannuation

title: TECHNOLOGY: AI advisory group has tech-linked $1 million funding
summary: The charity Good Ancestors received over $1 million in funding linked to Anthropic, an AI company, to influence Australia's AI and copyright policies. Critics argue this raises concerns about whether the charity truly represents Australian creators' interests or those of big tech.
ticker_line: Charity funding raises AI policy concerns
author: David Swan
heading: Regulation

title: Tasmania bets big on AI as Liberals chase ‘efficiency’ wins
summary: Tasmania's government is increasing its use of AI tools to improve efficiency and productivity in public services, with a budget of $4 million allocated for AI projects. However, concerns remain about transparency and the lack of a register to track AI usage across departments.
ticker_line: Tasmania boosts AI use in public services
author: Rob Inglis
heading: AI

title: Aussie state reeling from Iran war crisis
summary: The NSW economy's growth forecast has dropped from 2.5% to 1% for the next financial year due to the war in the Middle East, according to Treasurer Daniel Mookhey. He highlighted that higher inflation and interest rates are impacting spending more in NSW than in other states.
ticker_line: NSW economy growth forecast slashed amid war crisis
author: Nathan Schmidt
heading: Economy

title: RBA warns more rate rises needed in desperate inflation fight
summary: The Reserve Bank of Australia (RBA) has raised interest rates to 4.35% for the third time in a row to combat rising inflation, which is expected to remain above target due to the ongoing conflict in the Middle East. Economists predict at least one more rate hike by November, but not likely in June.
ticker_line: RBA raises rates again to fight inflation
author: Giuseppe Tauriello
heading: Economy

title: Banks quick to act on negative gearing
summary: Australia's banks are tightening rules on negative gearing after the government announced it will only apply to new investment properties from mid-2027. Macquarie Bank has already stopped considering negative gearing for established properties, leading to expectations of reduced investor loans and challenges for first-home buyers.
ticker_line: Banks tighten rules on negative gearing investments
author: Anthony Keane
heading: Banking

title: ASIC opens fire on former Rex execs
summary: The Australian Securities and Investments Commission (ASIC) is taking legal action against former executives of Rex Airlines for allegedly misleading the market about the company's financial health in early 2023. Four former directors, including the executive chairman, are accused of failing to disclose the true financial situation before the airline went into administration in July 2024.
ticker_line: ASIC targets Rex Airlines' former executives
heading: Regulation

title: UNSW AI Institute warns Australia risks losing AI productivity gains offshore
summary: The UNSW AI Institute warns that Australia could lose out on the economic benefits of artificial intelligence unless it invests in local capabilities and controls its data. Director Dr Sue Keay highlights the risk of relying on foreign companies for AI, which may limit local job growth and tax revenue.
ticker_line: Australia must invest in local AI capabilities
heading: AI

title: All eyes on RBA official for verdict on federal budget
summary: Investors are keenly awaiting a speech by RBA chief economist Sarah Hunter after the federal budget, which has sparked debate over its effects on inflation. Economists suggest the budget's impact is neutral but could lead to more interest rate hikes from the RBA.
ticker_line: Investors await RBA's take on federal budget
heading: Economy

title: HSBC agrees to $35m fine for scam failure
summary: HSBC Australia has agreed to pay a $35 million fine after failing to protect customers from scams that cost them their life savings. The bank admitted to serious shortcomings in its scam prevention measures and has already compensated affected customers.
ticker_line: HSBC fined $35 million for scam failures
author: Max Mason
heading: Banking

title: APRA grills Westpac on SME unit woes
summary: Westpac's small business division is under investigation by the Australian Prudential Regulation Authority (APRA) after an internal report revealed serious issues with their processes. Over 20 Westpac bankers, including senior managers, have been interviewed as part of APRA's wider review of lending practices for small and medium businesses across major banks.
ticker_line: APRA investigates Westpac's small business practices
author: Joyce Moullakis
heading: Banking

title: RBA and APRA put banks on 'war footing'
summary: Australia's financial regulators are urging banks to strengthen their defenses against foreign threats and cyberattacks due to rising geopolitical risks. They want banks to improve staff vetting and crisis management to protect the financial system from new challenges.
ticker_line: Regulators urge banks to boost security measures
author: James Eyers
heading: Banking

title: Street Talk: Bupa in $450m deal to buy Partnered Health
summary: Bupa has agreed to buy Partnered Health for about $450 million, expanding its healthcare services in Australia. The acquisition includes over 60 medical and skin cancer clinics, aiming to enhance Bupa's primary healthcare offerings.
ticker_line: Bupa invests $450m in Partnered Health acquisition
author: Sarah Thompson, Kanika Sood and Emm
heading: Health Insurance

title: Macquarie closing on $3bn deal for Vic land registry
summary: Macquarie Asset Management is close to a $3 billion deal to buy Secure Electronic Registries Victoria from Aware Super. This follows a competitive bidding process and comes amid concerns over changes to property tax rules that could affect the asset's value.
ticker_line: Macquarie nears $3bn land registry acquisition
author: DATA ROOM with BRIDGET CARTER
heading: Investments

title: Australian shares hit over 1-month low as oil prices amplify inflationary woes
summary: Australian shares fell to their lowest level in over a month, with the S&P/ASX 200 index down 1.5% due to declining mining and gold stocks. Rising oil prices, driven by global tensions, have raised inflation concerns, prompting the Reserve Bank of Australia to adjust its economic outlook.
ticker_line: Australian shares hit over 1-month low amid inflation fears
heading: Economy

title: ‘Unachievable scale’: Tasmania drops state insurer plan
summary: Tasmania has abandoned its plan to create a state-backed insurer called TasInsure due to concerns about its feasibility and high costs. Instead, the government will establish a new authority to work with existing insurers to improve insurance affordability and availability.
ticker_line: Tasmania drops state insurer plan for new approach
heading: General Insurance

title: ASIC home cover comparator visited less than 25 times a day
summary: The ASIC home and contents insurance comparison website is rarely used, averaging fewer than 25 visits a day. Despite a recent federal budget allocation of $1 million for its maintenance, the site has seen limited engagement since its launch over 10 years ago.
ticker_line: ASIC insurance comparison site sees low traffic
heading: Regulation

title: Swiss Re hunts for new country chief
summary: Swiss Re is looking for a new CEO for Australia and New Zealand after Trent Thomson was promoted to a global position. He will start his new role as head of global specialty in London on July 1, 2026.
ticker_line: Swiss Re seeks new ANZ CEO after promotion
heading: General Insurance

title: Queensland expands flood mitigation grants
summary: Queensland is expanding its Stronger Homes Grant to help more homeowners protect against flooding, offering up to $10,000 for upgrades. This includes those affected by recent floods and cyclones, with applications open until November 30.
ticker_line: Queensland boosts flood grant support for homeowners
heading: General Insurance

title: Libs flag ‘new tax’ fears over state ESL removal
summary: The NSW Labor government plans to remove the emergency services levy (ESL) from insurance and replace it with a new property levy, which they say could lower costs for many homeowners. However, the opposition warns this could lead to a new tax burden on families.
ticker_line: Concerns rise over potential new tax in NSW
heading: General Insurance

title: The networks we rely on face growing risk from natural disasters
summary: A new report warns that over $57 billion of Victoria's infrastructure is at risk from increasing natural disasters like bushfires and floods, with costs expected to rise to $71 billion by 2070. It highlights the need for urgent action to protect roads, rail, and energy assets to avoid higher recovery costs in the future.
ticker_line: Victoria's infrastructure faces $57 billion disaster risk
author: the climate team’s Alex Lim and Sac
heading: General Insurance

title: CBA rout just start of budget fallout
summary: Commonwealth Bank's shares fell 10.4% after the federal budget announced tax changes affecting property and lending. Analysts warn this could signal a decline in the bank's growth potential, raising concerns for investors.
ticker_line: CBA shares plunge after budget tax changes
author: David Rogers, Exchange
heading: Banking

title: AI agents are reshaping B2B marketing
summary: Artificial intelligence is changing B2B marketing by influencing how businesses research and choose products, leading to a new approach called agent-based marketing. Companies must adapt their strategies to include AI systems as part of the buying process and rethink how they engage with customers.
ticker_line: AI transforms B2B marketing strategies
author: Marissa Dacay
heading: AI

title: AI boom lifts risk to grid security
summary: Major electricity companies warn that the rapid growth of data centres, driven by AI, is increasing security risks to Australia's power grid, potentially leading to blackouts. New rules are being proposed to ensure these data centres maintain stability during power disturbances.
ticker_line: Data centres raise power grid security concerns
author: Perry Williams, Chief Business Corr
heading: AI

title: Interest rates: Contrarians tip more RBA raising
summary: Some economists believe the Reserve Bank of Australia may raise interest rates again, despite market expectations that rates have peaked. They warn that ongoing inflation and geopolitical issues could lead to further increases in borrowing costs.
ticker_line: Economists predict more RBA interest rate hikes ahead
author: Cecile Lefort
heading: Economy

title: ASIC says fund loan valuations lagging 'reality'
summary: The Australian Securities and Investments Commission (ASIC) is worried that private credit funds are not valuing their loans correctly as the sector faces economic stress. After reviewing 52 funds, ASIC found that many valuations do not reflect current market conditions, increasing risks for investors.
ticker_line: ASIC warns on private credit fund valuations
author: Jonathan Shapiro
heading: Regulation

title: ASIC's D-Day looms for private credit
summary: The Australian Securities and Investments Commission (ASIC) is urging private credit firms to update their asset valuations by June 30, 2026, to ensure accurate reporting and address concerns about the sector's stability amid economic pressures. ASIC has identified issues with disclosure and valuation processes and warns that failing to reflect economic realities in valuations could lead to serious problems for the industry.
ticker_line: ASIC demands action from private credit firms
author: James Thomson
heading: Regulation

title: Don’t repeat post-Cold War error: RBA
summary: The Reserve Bank's Brad Jones warns that Australia’s financial institutions must prepare for rising geopolitical threats, including cyber attacks, as complacency similar to the post-Cold War era could undermine the financial system's resilience. He emphasizes the need for stronger cyber security and better contingency planning to handle potential shocks.
ticker_line: RBA warns of rising geopolitical risks to finance
author: MATTHEW CRANSTON
heading: Banking

title: ASIC warns banks over return to wealth market
summary: Australia's corporate regulator, ASIC, has warned banks to avoid past mistakes as they consider returning to the wealth market. ASIC chair Sarah Court emphasized the importance of getting basic customer services right before pursuing new innovations.
ticker_line: ASIC warns banks on wealth market return
author: Cliona O’Dowd
heading: Banking

title: Narrow by-election win puts Qld Labor leader on notice
summary: Queensland Labor leader Steven Miles faces pressure after a narrow by-election win, with an 8% swing against Labor. The result raises questions about his leadership and the impact of federal budget cuts on state issues.
ticker_line: Labor leader Steven Miles under pressure after by-election
author: SARAH ELKS and NATASHA BITA
heading: General Insurance

title: 1.2 million Queensland homes unprepared as smoke alarm deadline nears
summary: With new smoke alarm laws starting January 1, 2027, about 1.2 million Queensland homes need upgrades to meet safety standards. Many homeowners are unaware of the requirements, risking non-compliance and potential insurance issues.
ticker_line: 1.2 million Queensland homes need smoke alarm upgrades
heading: General Insurance

title: Older Australians concerned about Labor's plan to reduce private health insurance rebates
summary: The Australian government plans to cut private health insurance rebates for older Australians, which could cost them an extra $226 to $255 a year. This change is expected to lead to 44,000 seniors dropping their insurance and may increase pressure on public hospitals.
ticker_line: Government cuts health rebates for older Australians
author: families Fiona Blackwood
heading: Health Insurance

title: Pride of our Footscray nightclub says it has been 'uninsurable' for two years
summary: Pride of our Footscray, a popular nightclub in Melbourne, has struggled to find affordable public liability insurance for two years, risking closure. The venue, known for its inclusive atmosphere, has faced skyrocketing premiums and is seeking government support to address the insurance crisis affecting live music venues.
ticker_line: Nightclub faces closure due to insurance crisis
heading: General Insurance

title: NAB taps Databricks' Genie AI tools to derive more value from its data
summary: National Australia Bank (NAB) is using Databricks' Genie AI tools to improve data analysis and speed up decision-making. The tools help create easy-to-query datasets, allowing staff to better understand customer needs and enhance service, particularly in dispute resolution.
ticker_line: NAB enhances data analysis with Databricks' AI tools
author: Ry Crozier
heading: Banking

title: Fixing doctors’ charges is simple: restore the patients’ rebates
summary: Federal Health Minister Mark Butler has highlighted the rising costs of specialist medical fees, urging for better transparency and a restoration of Medicare rebates to help patients manage out-of-pocket expenses. He criticizes past government actions that have failed to keep rebates in line with inflation, leading to increased financial strain on patients seeking medical care.
ticker_line: Health Minister calls for Medicare rebate restoration
author: MICHAEL GANNON
heading: Health Insurance

title: Unions, industry super oppose strict 12-year terms: APRA faces battle over board limits
summary: The Australian Prudential Regulation Authority (APRA) plans to enforce a strict 12-year limit on board directors at major financial institutions, despite strong opposition from unions and industry super funds who argue it lacks evidence and disrupts governance. The new rules, aimed at improving oversight, will take effect in January 2028 after further consultations.
ticker_line: APRA imposes 12-year limit on board directors
author: Lucas Baird
heading: Regulation

title: Two weeks to go and country still waits for details on 'new' super tax
summary: The government has not yet provided details on a new tax for high-value superannuation accounts, which will start in two weeks. This uncertainty affects many retirees, including former politicians, as financial advisers seek clarity from authorities who also lack information.
ticker_line: Government delays details on new super tax
author: Phillip Coorey
heading: Superannuation

title: Costs: Steep public liability bills mean 'fun stuff is going to get cancelled'
summary: Rising public liability insurance costs are threatening adventure tourism, music festivals, and volunteer activities in Australia, with some organisations facing increases of up to 168%. Business leaders warn that if this trend continues, many fun activities could be cancelled, similar to issues seen in the US.
ticker_line: Rising insurance costs threaten fun activities
author: Paul Karp
heading: General Insurance

title: Rebates: Private health bosses call for gold cover overhaul
summary: Private health leaders warn that 200,000 Australians may downgrade their health insurance due to rising costs and changes to tax rebates for seniors, which could strain public hospitals. They are calling for a simpler health cover system as the current four-tiered structure is seen as outdated and confusing.
ticker_line: Health leaders urge overhaul of insurance tiers
author: Michael Smith
heading: Health Insurance

title: Affordability: Suncorp chief slams NSW tax hit on home premiums
summary: Suncorp's CEO Lisa Harrison criticises the NSW government's emergency service tax, which makes up nearly half of home insurance premiums, saying it unfairly burdens customers. The government is reviewing this tax as many residents struggle with rising insurance costs.
ticker_line: Suncorp slams NSW tax on home insurance premiums
author: Lucy Slade
heading: General Insurance

title: Pressures: Soaring costs will bring double-digit premium rises
summary: Australia's biggest insurers warn that rising costs from natural disasters and construction inflation will lead to double-digit increases in insurance premiums each year. The government is pushing for better explanations from insurers about these price hikes and is considering new regulations to improve transparency.
ticker_line: Insurers predict double-digit premium hikes ahead
author: James Eyers and Joyce Moullakis
heading: General Insurance

title: Australia's financial industry should prepare for 'more shock-prone future', RBA warns
summary: Australia's financial industry must prepare for increased risks from foreign interference and cyber attacks, according to RBA official Brad Jones. He emphasized the need for better crisis planning and compliance with growing financial sanctions in a changing global landscape.
ticker_line: RBA warns of increased financial risks ahead
heading: Banking

title: David Littleproud calls for investigation into insurers in south-west Queensland
summary: David Littleproud, a federal MP, is urging an investigation into insurance companies in south-west Queensland after residents faced massive premium hikes of up to 500%. He claims the lack of competition is harming locals, making insurance unaffordable and impacting home ownership.
ticker_line: MP calls for insurance investigation in Queensland
author: Grace Nakamura and Aisling Brennan
heading: General Insurance

title: RBA walks the inflation tightrope with rate hold
summary: The Reserve Bank of Australia has kept interest rates at 4.35% to combat high inflation, which is currently at 4.2%. This decision aims to slow economic growth and increase unemployment, with forecasts suggesting growth may drop to 1.5% and unemployment could rise to nearly 5% over the next 18 months.
ticker_line: RBA holds rates to tackle high inflation
author: Duncan Evans
heading: Economy

title: Rebate reform ‘to hit elderly poor hardest’
summary: Medical and seniors groups are urging Prime Minister Anthony Albanese to reconsider plans to cut higher private health insurance rebates for over-65s, which could harm over three million older Australians and increase pressure on public hospitals. The government estimates this change will save $3 billion, but critics warn it may lead to worse health outcomes for low-income seniors.
ticker_line: Health groups oppose rebate cuts for seniors
author: STEPHEN LUNN SOCIAL AFFAI
heading: Health Insurance

title: ‘Sacred cow’: Tax change divides experts
summary: The Albanese government is changing tax rules for property investors to help more Australians buy homes, but experts are divided on whether it will work. Some say it will slow housing prices, while others warn it could reduce new home building.
ticker_line: Tax changes aim to boost home ownership
author: Cameron Micallef
heading: Economy

title: Economics joins the culture war
summary: Treasurer Jim Chalmers has proposed major tax reforms in the 2026 budget, including changes to capital gains tax and negative gearing, sparking a cultural debate on economics. Opposition Leader Angus Taylor plans to repeal these changes, leading to a significant divide in economic policy perspectives.
ticker_line: Tax reforms ignite cultural debate in Australia
author: Matthew Cranston
heading: Economy

title: Australian pensioners finding rent and cost-of-living relief in moving overseas
summary: Many Australian pensioners, like Peter Stevenson, are moving overseas to afford a better lifestyle as rising rents in Australia make it hard to live on their pensions. Experts say the government needs to improve support for renters to prevent more pensioners from leaving the country.
ticker_line: Pensioners seek affordable living abroad
author: families Fiona Blackwood
heading: Economy

title: Comment: ANZ and NAB join the chorus line on falling house prices
summary: ANZ and NAB have changed their forecasts, predicting that house prices in Australia will fall in 2026 due to a drop in investor loans and proposed tax changes. They expect significant declines, especially in Sydney and Melbourne, with overall property values likely to take years to recover.
ticker_line: ANZ and NAB predict falling house prices
author: Elizabeth Knight
heading: Economy

title: Strait peace pours oil on RBA’s troubled waters
summary: A peace deal between the US and Iran has lowered oil prices, which may help the Reserve Bank of Australia keep interest rates steady. However, economic challenges like rising unemployment and falling house prices remain a concern.
ticker_line: US-Iran deal eases oil prices, impacts RBA decisions
author: David Rogers, Exchange
heading: Economy

title: Aussies making AI work for them
summary: A new Microsoft report shows that while many Australian workers are using AI effectively, over two-thirds worry they are falling behind. Despite increased AI spending, most businesses have not yet seen a return on their investment.
ticker_line: Aussie workers embrace AI but fear falling behind
author: Eilidh Sproul-Mellis
heading: AI

title: ‘Tell him he’s dreaming’: Chalmers’ budget fuels housing crisis
summary: Treasurer Jim Chalmers' budget aims to tackle housing affordability and intergenerational inequality, but critics argue it will worsen the housing crisis as prices are expected to rise and support for first-home buyers may not be enough.
ticker_line: Chalmers' budget raises concerns over housing crisis
author: Noel Whittaker
heading: Economy

title: $500k repair bill: The kitchen time-bomb that could void your insurance
summary: Queensland homeowners are at risk of huge repair bills and insurance issues due to bursting flexible hoses, which can cause severe water damage. Many homeowners are unaware of the need for regular checks, with some damages reaching up to $500,000.
ticker_line: Queensland homeowners face flexi-hose repair risks
author: Tayla Couacaud
heading: General Insurance

title: SMSFs back in from cold for wealth accumulation
summary: Self-managed superannuation funds (SMSFs) are becoming popular again after changes in the budget that make other investment options less attractive. New taxes on trusts and capital gains mean SMSFs remain a tax-effective choice for many Australians.
ticker_line: SMSFs regain popularity amid new tax changes
author: Lucas Baird
heading: Superannuation

title: QBE launches QuickQuote workers’ comp platform
summary: QBE Insurance has launched QuickQuote, a new digital platform for brokers to quickly quote and issue workers' compensation policies for small and medium-sized businesses. The service became available on April 30, 2026, in several Australian regions for policies under $50,000.
ticker_line: QBE introduces QuickQuote for faster workers' comp quotes
author: Coverager
heading: General Insurance

title: Allan dismisses fears that state lacks power for data centre surge
summary: Victorian Premier Jacinta Allan reassured that the state's renewable energy investments will support the growing demand from data centres, despite concerns about energy supply. Data centre power use in Victoria has surged, with a forecast that it could consume one-eighth of the state's electricity in ten years.
ticker_line: Victoria backs renewable energy for data centres
author: Sumeyya Ilanbey and Jenny Wiggins
heading: AI

title: ‘Overvalued’: Why the CBA rout may worsen
summary: Commonwealth Bank's shares fell sharply after the government announced tax changes, leading analysts to believe the stock is overvalued and may drop further. Despite a small rebound, experts warn of ongoing risks to the bank's growth and valuation.
ticker_line: CBA shares plunge amid tax change fears
author: DAVID ROGERS EXCHANGE
heading: Banking

title: Family support to be considered before NDIS funds granted
summary: New legislation will require people to complete all appropriate treatments before accessing NDIS funds, considering family support and other services. The changes aim to reduce NDIS growth to 0.2% over two years and save $185 billion over the next decade.
ticker_line: New NDIS rules focus on treatment and family support
author: SARAH ISON, SENIOR POLITICAL REPORT
heading: General Insurance

title: Suncorp ‘explored every other option’ before quitting tobacco retailers
summary: Suncorp has stopped insuring tobacconists due to high risks and illegal activities. The company also withdrew from some island insurance markets after Cyclone Debbie, citing rebuilding costs and storm severity as key factors.
ticker_line: Suncorp exits tobacco insurance market amid risks
heading: General Insurance

title: Old tech: warning APS exposed to cyber threats
summary: A report warns that outdated technology in Australian government agencies could cost $1.4 billion annually and make them vulnerable to cyber threats, especially from future quantum computing. Agencies must plan to upgrade their systems by the end of 2026 to protect sensitive data.
ticker_line: Outdated tech poses $1.4 billion risk to Australia
heading: Regulation

title: Soaring costs fuel retirees’ fears
summary: The cost of a comfortable retirement for couples has risen to nearly $80,000 a year, up 26% in five years, mainly due to inflation. Many retirees and pre-retirees are worried about their finances, with some planning to work past age 70 as living costs continue to climb.
ticker_line: Retirement costs surge, raising financial fears
author: Anthony Keane
heading: Superannuation

title: Allan’s extra spending in Victoria is 10 times Albanese’s nationally
summary: The Victorian government plans to spend $6 billion more over the next three years compared to the federal government's $600 million, raising concerns about inflation. The extra spending in Victoria is seen as a challenge to the federal government's efforts to control rising prices.
ticker_line: Victoria's spending outpaces federal budget tenfold
author: Sumeyya Ilanbey
heading: Economy

title: Matos means business: ANZ’s new bonus shake-up
summary: ANZ's CEO Nuno Matos is changing the bank's bonus system to link payments to new corporate values, aiming to improve culture and performance. This will affect thousands of managers and is part of a broader strategy to enhance accountability and reduce costs.
ticker_line: ANZ revamps bonus system to boost culture
author: Eric Johnston
heading: Banking

title: Most AI Initiatives miss Expected ROI - ISACA
summary: A new report from ISACA reveals that most businesses are not seeing the expected returns on their artificial intelligence (AI) investments, despite widespread adoption. Ninety percent of digital trust professionals express concerns about the effectiveness and safety of these AI initiatives.
ticker_line: Most AI projects fail to deliver expected returns
heading: AI

title: Optimistic forecasts questioned
summary: Treasury's optimistic forecasts for wage growth and inflation are being questioned as real wages fall and inflation remains high. Economists warn that government spending could complicate efforts to control inflation and interest rates.
ticker_line: Treasury's wage growth forecasts face scrutiny
author: Lea Jurkovic | Economics correspond
heading: Economy

title: Housing's 30-year super cycle is over
summary: The Australian government's recent budget changes to capital gains tax and negative gearing are expected to end a 30-year housing investment boom, making property less attractive for investors and potentially lowering house prices.
ticker_line: End of housing investment boom signals major changes
author: James Thomson
heading: Economy

title: Bad debt fears help carve $30b off CBA
summary: Commonwealth Bank shares dropped 10.4% after disappointing earnings and fears that new tax changes will hurt loan demand. The bank's cash profit of $2.7 billion missed expectations, leading to concerns about rising bad debts and increased provisions for loans.
ticker_line: CBA shares plunge amid bad debt fears
author: James Eyers
heading: Banking

title: Trump's war is costing the budget billions - and counting
summary: The ongoing war between the US and Iran has cost the US government an extra $15 billion over four years, affecting Australia's economy by slowing growth and increasing inflation due to rising oil prices, which could reach $200 a barrel if the conflict continues.
ticker_line: War with Iran costs billions, slows Australian growth
author: Matthew Knott and Mike Foley
heading: Economy

title: Tasmanian government breaks election promise over TasInsure as 'insurance company' overhauled
summary: The Tasmanian government has changed its plan for TasInsure, a state-owned insurance agency, now focusing on overseeing the insurance market instead of directly providing insurance. Premier Jeremy Rockliff stated this new approach aims to improve affordability and competition in the insurance sector.
ticker_line: Tasmanian government alters TasInsure insurance plan
author: state political Josh Dugga
heading: General Insurance

title: Bid budget changes tipped to put the brakes on rising house prices
summary: The Albanese government's property tax overhaul is expected to slow house price growth and shift investor focus to new builds, but may lead to short-term price drops and increased rents due to market sentiment.
author: Shane Wright and Natassia Chrysanth
heading: Economy

title: CBA the first casualty of Chalmers’ budget
summary: Commonwealth Bank's share price plummeted over 10% following Treasurer Jim Chalmers' budget, which introduced significant tax changes affecting investor property loans and raised concerns about economic uncertainty.
author: Eric Johnston, Associate
heading: Banking

title: State to tap money markets for more than $100bn
summary: Victoria plans to borrow over $100 billion in the next four years, leading to annual interest costs exceeding $7 billion as it faces a significant refinancing burden from maturing low-interest pandemic-era bonds.
author: Anthony Galloway, Victorian politic
heading: Economy

title: Banks’ negative gearing reaction
summary: Australia's banks faced significant share price declines following the government's decision to end new negative gearing arrangements and reduce capital gains tax relief, with Commonwealth Bank shares dropping 10.4% amid a weak profit report.
author: David Ross
heading: Banking

title: SMSF investors score surprise win
summary: The 2026 budget maintains favourable tax treatment for self-managed super funds (SMSFs), allowing them to borrow and benefit from advantageous capital gains tax rules compared to personal investments.
author: JAMES KIRBY, ANALYSIS
heading: Superannuation

title: Super revamp may invite higher risks
summary: The Albanese government plans to revamp the superannuation performance test to encourage investment in riskier assets, aiming to enhance retirement outcomes while addressing concerns over recent fund failures.
author: Cliona O’Dowd
heading: Superannuation

title: Coalition vow to cut off welfare to non-citizens
summary: The Coalition plans to restrict welfare access for new migrants until they become Australian citizens, aiming to save billions and address concerns over migrant integration.
author: James Morrow
heading: Health Insurance

title: Healthcare M&A winners, losers
summary: The federal budget has negatively impacted Healius with a profit downgrade and no new funding for pathology, while ForHealth benefits from $1.8bn in funding for urgent care clinics.
author: BRIDGET CARTER, DATA ROOM
heading: Health Insurance

title: Angus Taylor using NDIS cuts as ‘pawn in bigger chess game’, Mark Butler warns
summary: Health Minister Mark Butler is pushing to pass controversial changes to the NDIS this month, despite opposition from the Coalition and Greens who may extend inquiries into the reforms. The changes could cut payments for over 200,000 participants and save the government $37.8 billion by 2030.
ticker_line: NDIS changes face opposition in Parliament
author: Sarah Basford Canales
heading: Regulation

title: Aussies can bank on knowing what the RBA will do next
summary: Recent actions by banks, like ANZ and Macquarie cutting interest rates, suggest they may not expect further rate hikes from the RBA. Analysts warn that rising unemployment and economic pressures could lead the RBA to lower rates soon to avoid a recession.
ticker_line: Banks signal possible RBA rate cuts ahead
author: Tim McIntyre
heading: Banking

title: Prolonged pause on interest rates a possibility as RBA board meets for June
summary: Economists expect the Reserve Bank of Australia to keep interest rates at 4.35% during its June meeting, with predictions of a prolonged pause on rate changes until 2027 due to mixed economic signals and high inflation. Some experts still foresee potential rate hikes later this year, but most believe cuts won't occur until 2027.
ticker_line: RBA likely to hold interest rates steady
author: business Alison Branley
heading: Economy

title: ASIC probes DroneShield chief’s share sale
summary: ASIC is investigating share sales by former DroneShield executives, including Oleg Vornik, following a $70 million sale that raised investor concerns.

heading: Regulation

title: Labor dials up $120m autism hotline plan
summary: Labor's 2026 budget introduces a $120 million autism hotline and various initiatives to support children with mild to moderate autism outside the NDIS, while seeking state contributions for funding.
author: SARAH ISON
heading: Health Insurance

title: Healthcare reps 'shocked' by 'unavoidable' comment after hospital violence
summary: Healthcare unions in the ACT are expressing concern over comments made by Health Minister Rachel Stephen-Smith regarding occupational violence, arguing that such violence is not an unavoidable aspect of healthcare work.
author: Lanie Tindale
heading: Health Insurance

title: Urgent clinics get shot in arm
summary: The 2026 Federal Budget allocates significant funding for healthcare, including $1.8bn for Medicare urgent care clinics and $25bn for public hospitals, while addressing Medicare fraud and adjusting private health insurance rebates.
author: Sarah Booth
heading: Health Insurance

title: Doctors brace for Medicare fraud blitz
summary: The Australian government plans to enhance Medicare compliance to recover $230 million annually from fraud, while also funding various health initiatives including permanent Medicare Urgent Care Clinics and adult dental services.
author: NATASHA ROBINSON, HEALTH
heading: Health Insurance

title: Is it time to reimagine risk management?
summary: Geoffrey Kovesdy from Deloitte & Touche LLP suggests that AI is prompting a fundamental rethinking of risk management practices, advocating for a redesign of risk functions rather than incremental changes.
author: Isobel Markham
heading: AI

title: Tech boss backs Aussie AI amid tax fears
summary: Amazon Web Services' new Australian managing director, Chris Casey, expresses confidence in local companies becoming AI leaders despite concerns over tax reforms, while highlighting the need for skills development and announcing a $20bn investment in AI infrastructure.
author: Jared Lynch
heading: AI

title: Massive problem budget failed to fix
summary: Australia's gross debt is set to exceed $1 trillion despite significant budget reforms aimed at curbing negative gearing and capital gains tax, with projected debt rising to $1.249 trillion by 2028.
author: Cameron Micallef
heading: Economy

title: Surfside residents fear homes could be lost to sea in next storm
summary: Residents of Surfside Beach, NSW, are worried their homes could be lost to the sea due to erosion and lack of effective government action. Despite previous emergency measures, many feel urgent solutions are needed as storms continue to threaten their properties.
ticker_line: Surfside residents fear homes lost to erosion
heading: General Insurance

title: Australia has valuable cards to play in the AI future, but critical gaps remain, new report finds
summary: A new report from the Tech Policy Design Institute highlights Australia's strong potential in artificial intelligence, suggesting it could become a key data centre hub and leverage its mineral resources. However, it warns that Australia must improve its data regulations and focus on renewable energy for data centres.
ticker_line: Australia's AI potential revealed in new report
author: business Gareth Hutchens
heading: AI

title: Eye on the storm: IAG takes bet on AI
summary: Insurance Australia Group (IAG) is investing in AI technology, including a start-up that automates insurance claims based on weather data. This move aims to improve efficiency and prepare for future risks in the insurance industry.
ticker_line: IAG invests in AI for automated insurance claims
author: David Ross
heading: General Insurance

title: Chalmers unveils biggest attack on wealth since federation
summary: The 2026 Chalmers budget introduces significant changes to capital gains tax and negative gearing, impacting housing prices and wealth distribution in Australia.
author: Robert Gottliebsen
heading: Economy

title: Treasury warns of inflation surge, growth slide
summary: Australia's Treasury warns of potential inflation surging to 7.25% and negative GDP growth due to ongoing geopolitical tensions, while a more optimistic scenario predicts inflation halving to 2.5% next year.
author: MATTHEW CRANSTON , THOMAS HENRY
heading: Economy

title: WA opposition slams state budget as ‘reckless and unsophisticated’
summary: The WA opposition has condemned the 2026-27 state budget as reckless and unsophisticated, arguing it fails to address inflation and economic challenges.
author: Holly Thompson
heading: Economy

title: Stop training people for yesterday’s jobs: AI demands we redesign work
summary: The shift towards AI as a general-purpose capability necessitates a redesign of work processes before implementing reskilling initiatives to enhance productivity and business value.
author: Elise Sharpley
heading: AI

title: ISACA poll finds AI adoption outpaces governance and ROI
summary: A recent ISACA survey reveals that while AI adoption is widespread, many organisations struggle with governance, ROI visibility, and incident response related to AI systems.
heading: AI

title: Hackers winning cyber war
summary: Australia's cybersecurity landscape is deteriorating, with a significant rise in severe vulnerabilities identified by ethical hackers, highlighting the increasing sophistication of cyber threats.
author: Eilidh Sproul-Mellis
heading: AI

title: Zurich Australia partners with Crop Risk Underwriting
summary: Zurich Australia has teamed up with Crop Risk Underwriting to offer crop insurance starting June 1, 2026. This partnership will provide coverage for farmers in several states against risks like hail and fire, using advanced data and AI technology.
ticker_line: Zurich partners with Crop Risk for insurance
author: Gabriella Ben-Hutta
heading: General Insurance

title: Bupa renews Blues deal amid saga
summary: Bupa has renewed its sponsorship deal with Carlton Football Club for three years, despite the ongoing scandal involving former president Luke Sayers. The new agreement includes clauses to protect Bupa's reputation amid concerns over the club's governance.
ticker_line: Bupa extends sponsorship with Carlton amid scandal
author: Christine Lacy
heading: Health Insurance

title: 'Significant risk': Disability ministers defy government's radical overhaul of the NDIS
summary: Disability ministers from all states are opposing the Albanese government's proposed changes to the NDIS, warning it could lead to many people with disabilities being left without proper support. The government aims to cut $38 billion in spending, but critics fear this will harm vulnerable Australians and shift costs to state services.
ticker_line: Disability ministers unite against NDIS overhaul
author: Broede Carmody and Mike Foley
heading: Regulation

title: Joyce’s crazy plan for the Reserve Bank would only make things worse
summary: Barnaby Joyce suggests the Reserve Bank should influence government spending to control inflation, but critics warn this could undermine its independence and lead to poor economic decisions. Joyce's ideas echo past mistakes seen in the UK, where government policies clashed with central bank goals.
ticker_line: Joyce's plan could harm Reserve Bank's role
author: Shane Wright
heading: Economy

title: Labor shelves private hospital pricing shake-up plan
summary: The Labor government has dropped plans for a new pricing model for private hospitals after pushback from Ramsay Health Care. Instead, they will consult on changes to health insurance products and services to improve the private healthcare system.
ticker_line: Labor abandons private hospital pricing reform plan
author: Michael Smith
heading: Health Insurance

title: Big Aussie short begins, major banks targeted
summary: Hedge funds are betting against Australia's big four banks, increasing their short positions to nearly $11 billion due to concerns over high valuations and a housing market slump caused by new tax rules. This is the largest short position recorded since 2010, with the Commonwealth Bank being the most targeted.
ticker_line: Hedge funds target big four banks with record shorts
author: Ben Graham
heading: Banking

title: ANZ urges back to future for banks
summary: ANZ CEO Nuno Matos says Australian banks should return to wealth management and insurance to boost profits, as their return on capital has dropped significantly. He also noted that recent government policies may slow mortgage market growth but aim to improve housing affordability.
ticker_line: ANZ calls for banks to re-enter wealth management
author: Angira Bharadwaj and Jonathan Shapi
heading: Banking

title: Westpac investor loans plunge 20pc on federal budget tax shock
summary: Westpac has seen a 20% drop in housing investor loan applications following proposed federal budget changes to negative gearing and capital gains tax. The bank expects house prices to fall further, while it aims to attract younger customers and improve its retail banking strategy.
ticker_line: Westpac's investor loans fall 20% amid tax changes
author: James Eyers
heading: Banking

title: One in five taking drugs for mental health treatment
summary: Nearly 20% of Australians are now prescribed mental health drugs, with ADHD treatments for boys tripling in the last decade. Experts are concerned about the ease of access to these medications and the lack of proper follow-up assessments.
ticker_line: Surge in mental health drug prescriptions raises concerns
author: Michael Smith | Health
heading: Health Insurance

title: AI safety ignored in sprint to go public, critics warn
summary: Australian super funds are investing heavily in AI companies like Anthropic and OpenAI as they prepare for IPOs, but critics warn that safety concerns about AI technology are being overlooked in the rush for profits. Recent developments, including Anthropic's new model launch, raise questions about the balance between innovation and safety.
ticker_line: Super funds chase AI IPO profits amid safety concerns
author: Paul Smith
heading: Investments

title: Westpac tips shift in home lending
summary: Westpac is changing its home lending strategy due to a drop in applications after the government's budget changes. The bank expects more interest-only loans as it aims to improve customer service and increase its share of the mortgage market.
ticker_line: Westpac shifts home lending strategy post-budget changes
author: David Ross
heading: Banking

title: Regulations stifling growth, says ANZ boss
summary: ANZ CEO Nuno Matos says Australia's strict regulations are slowing economic growth and urges a more entrepreneurial approach. He also anticipates a decline in home lending due to recent government tax changes.
ticker_line: ANZ CEO warns of growth limits from regulations
author: David Ross
heading: Economy

title: Mums’ desperate calls highlight housing crisis
summary: A charity in Northland reports a rise in calls from mothers needing emergency housing to avoid living under bridges with their children. New research shows over 33,000 children in New Zealand face severe housing issues, with many in Northland living in overcrowded or unsafe conditions.
ticker_line: Northland mothers face housing crisis for kids
author: Yolisa Tswanya
heading: Economy

title: How to boost a super nest egg
summary: Superannuation contribution caps have increased, allowing Australians to boost their retirement savings significantly. Individuals can contribute up to $1.4 million over five years, with various strategies available to maximise tax benefits.
ticker_line: Superannuation caps rise, boost retirement savings potential
author: Anthony Keane
heading: Superannuation

title: Age pension may be next on the hit list
summary: The government may change age pension access rules to promote fairness, potentially including the family home in means tests. A report suggests this could save $10 billion and affect many retirees who currently receive the pension despite having significant assets.
ticker_line: Government considers changes to age pension access
author: James Kirby
heading: Economy

title: Artificial intelligence: OpenAI targets booming Aussie market with ChatGPT upgrade
summary: OpenAI has launched ChatGPT Work, a new version of its AI tool designed to automate work tasks and integrate with popular applications like Gmail and Slack. This upgrade aims to help users complete projects more efficiently, reflecting the growing demand for AI in Australian workplaces.
ticker_line: OpenAI unveils ChatGPT Work for Aussie market
author: Jessica Yun
heading: AI

title: Jim Chalmers is going for broke. Will we end up a poorer country?
summary: Treasurer Jim Chalmers is set to unveil a significant budget aimed at tax reform amidst rising inflation and economic challenges, with proposals that may impact intergenerational equity and investment strategies.
author: Matthew Cranston
heading: Economy

title: Commonwealth Bank shareholders go to High Court seeking damages for inflated share prices
summary: Commonwealth Bank shareholders are in the High Court seeking damages for inflated share prices due to the bank's failure to report over 53,000 large transactions properly. They argue that this lack of transparency led to them paying too much for their shares, while the bank claims the shareholders have not proven their losses.
ticker_line: CBA shareholders seek damages over share price inflation
heading: Banking

title: Stocks recover ground after early dive
summary: The Australian sharemarket recovered from a sharp opening drop, with the S&P/ASX 200 closing down 20.9 points at 8604.2. Strong performances from telecommunications and supermarket stocks helped offset losses in the materials sector, particularly iron ore producers.
ticker_line: ASX recovers after early losses on Tuesday
author: CAMERON MICALLEF, EQUITIES
heading: Investments

title: ASIC blasts super fund payout delays
summary: The Australian Securities & Investments Commission (ASIC) is concerned about superannuation funds delaying death benefit payments to grieving members. Despite some improvements, many trustees are still not meeting their responsibilities, and ASIC may take legal action if the situation does not improve.
ticker_line: ASIC warns super funds on death benefit delays
author: Angelica Snowden
heading: Superannuation

title: Climate change costs residents
summary: A report shows that climate change has cost NSW residents about $20,000 each per year, with future impacts expected to worsen household budgets through job losses and higher grocery prices. By 2070, the economy could shrink by $1.5 trillion if climate change continues unchecked.
ticker_line: NSW residents face $20,000 annual climate cost
author: Caitlin Fitzsimmons | Climate and e
heading: Economy

title: Labor slammed on NDIS cutbacks
summary: Labor's proposed changes to the NDIS are facing strong backlash from Paralympians, doctors, and disability advocates, who warn that the reforms could reduce support for disabled Australians and force parents to medicate their children to access the scheme. A Senate inquiry revealed no support for the changes, with calls for the government to either scrap or significantly amend the bill before it is passed.
ticker_line: Labor's NDIS reforms face major backlash
author: Sarah Ison, Senior political report
heading: Regulation

title: ‘The window is now’: Microsoft pitches solution to AI grid crisis
summary: Microsoft plans to invest $25 billion in Australia over three years, urging the government to create an 'AI in Energy Fund' to address rising electricity demand from data centres. The company warns that without changes to the power grid, Australia could face supply issues and increased costs for consumers.
ticker_line: Microsoft pushes for energy fund amid AI boom
author: Perry Williams
heading: Investments

title: Westpac brings forward RBA rate cut forecasts, after one more hike
summary: Coles is in talks to buy Greencross, a pet-care business, from TPG Capital for about $4 billion. This move could impact investors by limiting their ability to invest in standalone pet care companies, raising concerns about competition in the market.
ticker_line: Coles eyes $4bn Greencross acquisition
author: David Rogers, Valerina Changarathil
heading: Investments

title: Hanson offers an ‘eternity of pain’
summary: Angus Taylor, the Liberal leader, warns that a One Nation government would worsen Australia's economy, increasing inflation and debt. He criticizes their policies as unfunded and potentially harmful, while Senator Hanson’s team dismisses his claims as ineffective.
ticker_line: Taylor warns of economic pain under One Nation
author: GREG BROWN, Chief Political Corresp
heading: Economy

title: Next RBA move will be rate cut, but timing’s uncertain: NAB
summary: Apple CEO Tim Cook announced new AI features for Siri at his last Worldwide Developers Conference, but shares fell nearly 2% as no major surprises were revealed. The new Siri will have enhanced capabilities and a new look, but some features will be limited by cost and geography.

author: Valerina Changarathil
heading: AI

title: CBA scrambles, new $1.4 trillion bombshell
summary: The AI industry is buzzing as OpenAI and Anthropic take steps to go public, which is causing a surge in interest from Australian investors, overwhelming Commonwealth Bank’s CommSec platform. This comes as SpaceX prepares for a historic IPO that could create many new billionaires.

author: Ben Graham
heading: Investments

title: Medical imaging provider to investigate improper Medicare billing allegations
summary: Integral Diagnostics will investigate claims of improper Medicare billing at Kalgoorlie Health Campus after concerns were raised about incorrect practices. The WA health minister has referred the matter to the state's corruption watchdog for further examination.
ticker_line: Investigation into Medicare billing practices launched
heading: Health Insurance

title: ‘Gut punch’ warning over the new rates reality
summary: Australian mortgage holders face permanently higher interest rates due to ongoing economic shocks, according to Oxford Economics. Despite recent inflation drops, the Reserve Bank is expected to keep rates steady for now as the economy slows.

author: Cameron Micallef
heading: Economy

title: Minister on board to slow AI juggernaut
summary: The Australian government supports Anthropic's call for a global pause in AI development to address safety concerns, as the company warns of risks from advanced AI that can improve itself. Assistant Minister Andrew Charlton emphasizes the need for careful regulation and safety measures as AI technology rapidly evolves.

author: Jared Lynch
heading: AI

title: Should older workers still be expected to do night shifts?
summary: The article discusses whether older hospital workers should be exempt from night shifts due to the increasing demands of after-hours work. Experts suggest balancing fairness and safety, advocating for a management approach that considers age without stereotyping abilities.
ticker_line: Debate on night shifts for older hospital workers
author: Jonathan Rivett
heading: Health Insurance

title: A $565 insurance saving in the regions could sting rich households
summary: NSW plans to save flood-prone households $565 a year on insurance by shifting costs to high-value properties, which could lead to richer households paying more. The Insurance Council supports this change to a fairer land tax system.

author: Paul Karp
heading: General Insurance

heading: Investments

title: Angus Taylor says One Nation policies would bring 'eternity of pain' to economy
summary: Opposition leader Angus Taylor criticises One Nation's economic plan, claiming it could cost Australia a trillion dollars and lead to higher interest rates and national debt. He warns that if Pauline Hanson gains power, it would result in significant economic pain for Australians.
ticker_line: Taylor warns One Nation policies could harm economy
author: political Holly Tregenza
heading: Economy

title: Industry urges Albanese government to relent on its ban on SMSF loans
summary: The Albanese government faces pressure to reconsider its ban on new self-managed super fund (SMSF) loans for residential property, which could negatively impact the housing market and construction projects. Industry experts argue that the ban contradicts the government's goals for increasing housing supply and may lead SMSFs to invest more in established properties instead of new developments.

author: Harrison Astbury
heading: Superannuation

title: Australians less satisfied with life than during pandemic as financial pressures mount
summary: Australians are feeling less satisfied with life now than during the COVID-19 pandemic, mainly due to rising financial pressures. Data shows life satisfaction dropped from 7.2 out of 10 in 2020 to 7.1 last year, with many struggling to manage costs and save money.
ticker_line: Australians report lower life satisfaction amid financial stress

heading: Economy

title: Long delays leave Tasmanians waiting for surgery amid worries about $700m in health cuts
summary: Tasmania's elective surgery waitlist has reached about 9,500 people, with many waiting years for procedures. The state government plans to cut $702 million from health services over four years, raising concerns about the impact on patient care and service delivery.

heading: Health

title: Data centre boom towns ride the economic wave, but can it last?
summary: Victoria and NSW are experiencing a massive data centre boom, investing nearly $300 billion, surpassing the previous mining boom. However, experts warn that this growth may not lead to long-term economic benefits for Australians and could face challenges ahead.
ticker_line: Data centre boom surpasses mining investment in Australia

heading: Economy

title: Westpac extends ‘DIDO’ banking
summary: Westpac is expanding its drive-in-drive-out banking service to six new towns in Queensland, aiming to provide in-person banking in areas without branches. The bank will also invest $10 million to improve branches in regional Queensland.

heading: Banking

title: NEW RESEARCH REVEALS HIDDEN SCALE OF CHILD HOMELESSNESS
summary: New research shows over 33,000 children in Aotearoa face severe housing deprivation, leading to higher risks of illness, school absenteeism, and contact with the justice system. The Coalition to End Women’s Homelessness emphasizes the need for housing solutions, especially for mothers, to protect these vulnerable children.
ticker_line: Over 33,000 children face severe housing issues

heading: General Insurance

The Coalition to End Women’s Homelessness highlights the need for housing solutions, especially for mothers, to support these vulnerable children.

title: Data centres an inflation threat: RBA
summary: The Reserve Bank of Australia is worried that the rapid growth of data centre investments could lead to competition for workers and resources, making it harder to control inflation and keep interest rates down. With tech companies investing heavily, including a $21.6 billion plan by Anthropic for data centre capacity, the RBA fears this could worsen existing skills shortages and capacity issues in the economy.
